acceptance:
1. internal/routing.Candidate has a typed FilterReason field (enum or string-typed-from-typed-constants).
2. service_routing.go has no string-matching classifier; routeCandidateFromInternal maps the typed internal value directly to the public FilterReason* constant.
3. New test go test ./internal/routing -run 'TestCandidateFilterReasonAtRejectionSite' passes: each rejection site (context window, tool support, reasoning, unhealthy/quota) sets the correct typed value at the time of rejection, not derived later.
4. Existing TestRouteCandidateFilterReasonClassification updated to use typed inputs; passes.
5. go test ./... -count=1 passes.
description:
Reviewer minor finding from agent-ddcc903b post-merge review. service_routing.go's deriveFilterReason classifier matches substrings of free-form Reason text emitted by the internal routing engine — a future engine wording tweak (e.g. 'ctx window' vs 'context window') silently demotes the rejection to FilterReasonScoredBelowTop and the public API loses fidelity.

Surface FilterReason as a typed field on internal/routing.Candidate (e.g. an enum: ContextTooSmall, NoToolSupport, ReasoningUnsupported, Unhealthy, ScoredBelowTop, Eligible) populated at the call site that decides why a candidate is rejected. service_routing.routeCandidateFromInternal becomes a one-line passthrough instead of a string parser.

In-scope files:
- internal/routing/engine.go (Candidate struct + filter call sites)
- internal/routing/score.go (any rejection setters)
- service_routing.go (deriveFilterReason replaced with passthrough)
- service_adr005_test.go (TestRouteCandidateFilterReasonClassification updated to drive on the typed enum, not strings)

Out of scope: any change to the public FilterReason* string constants in service.go (those are the public surface; only the internal mapping changes).
title: Make FilterReason a typed field on routing.Candidate (drop string-matching classifier)

description:
The architecture review established the target doctrine: cmd/agent is a first-party consumer of the public DdxAgent service contract; service owns config-backed routing, provider construction, native failover, event normalization, and session-log persistence; provider adapters and internal/core stay behind the service boundary. The current docs were stale and partly permissive: CONTRACT-003 described cmd/ddx-agent as a transitional adapter with an internal-import allowlist; SD-002 still said the CLI constructs providers and calls agent.Run(); architecture.md described the older agent.Run/provider-package shape; SD-005 still described provider construction in terms of passing one concrete Provider to agent.Run().

Finalize those docs so they state the correct architecture precisely and consistently. Explicitly document that PreResolved is only for RouteDecision values produced by ResolveRoute, not CLI-reconstructed candidate lists; document that SessionLogDir controls storage location but session-log persistence is service-owned; document that callers never receive provider instances.

In-scope files:
- docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-002-standalone-cli.md
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-005-provider-config.md
- docs/helix/02-design/architecture.md
- contributor guidance if needed to state the boundary clearly

Out of scope:
- Code changes beyond the minimum needed to keep docs/tests aligned
- Deleting CLI routing/session-log code (separate child beads)
title: Finalize the service-boundary specs for CLI, service, and provider ownership

acceptance: (1) reasoning_byte_limit and reasoning_stall_timeout are configurable in config.yaml (provider or global level). (2) Defaults raised to 256KB and 300s. (3) 0 means unlimited for both, matching max_iterations pattern. (4) Error messages include model name and current threshold value. (5) go test ./... passes; stream_consume_test.go covers configurable-limit paths.
description:
Hardcoded constants in stream_consume.go abort model responses prematurely for local models that use extended thinking:

- `reasoningByteLimit = 32 * 1024` (32KB) — aborts if pure reasoning_content exceeds 32K chars before any content delta
- `reasoningStallTimeout = 120 * time.Second` — aborts if only reasoning tokens arrive for 2 minutes

Observed failures:
- google/gemma-4-31b on LM Studio: reasoning overflow at 32KB on a small P3 bead
- qwen3.5-27b on LM Studio: reasoning stall on same bead
- Both models use extended thinking by default; coding tasks routinely produce >32KB of reasoning before the first content token
- Local models at 5-15 tok/s can easily spend 2+ minutes reasoning — 120s is normal behavior, not a stall
- qwen/qwen3-coder-next does NOT hit this (emits content tokens earlier)

## Fix scope

1. Make both thresholds configurable via config.yaml (reasoning_byte_limit, reasoning_stall_timeout)
2. Raise defaults: 256KB byte limit, 300s stall timeout
3. Allow 0 = unlimited for both (same pattern as max_iterations)
4. Include model name and current threshold in error messages

Relevant files: stream_consume.go (lines 13, 18), config/config.go.

GitHub issue: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent/issues/1
resolution: fixed: reasoning_byte_limit and reasoning_stall_timeout configurable in config.yaml, defaults raised to 256KB/300s
title: fix: make reasoning stall/overflow thresholds configurable and raise defaults

acceptance:
1. Service final events expose normalized final response text through a stable public field or public method; old raw text_delta behavior remains available for one migration window.
2. Native Service.Execute maps agent result output to the normalized final text.
3. Codex, Claude, and at least one of pi, opencode, or gemini map existing stream aggregate final text into the normalized final text without exposing raw stream-frame envelopes.
4. A DDx-style reviewer verdict extraction regression parses APPROVE/BLOCK text from the normalized field and does not inspect raw codex item.completed JSON.
5. CONTRACT-003 documents the field or method plus migration guidance.
6. go test . ./internal/harnesses/... passes.
description:
Surfaced by DDx consumer ddx-f7ae036f. DDx's post-merge reviewer currently parses raw harness stream frames to extract the reviewer's final verdict text. For codex that means reading `{"type":"item.completed", ...}` envelopes directly. That's agent-owned stream format; consumers shouldn't depend on it.

## Concrete failure

DDx's verdict-extraction regex anchors on the reviewer's text, but the reviewer's text is embedded inside codex's stream-frame JSON. When the frame shape shifts (v0.6.0→v0.7.0, or per-harness variation), the regex silently misses and ParseReviewVerdict defaults to BLOCK. An APPROVE-verdicted review gets recorded as BLOCK; the bead reopens; the loop re-runs it; the reviewer APPROVEs again; same silent mis-extract. Cost-visible wasted throughput.

## Proposal

Expose the final cleaned response text from the harness at the service boundary. Two possible shapes — pick whichever fits the existing ExecuteResponse contract:

1. `ExecuteResponse.FinalText string` — the harness's user-facing final message, already stripped of stream-frame wrapping, reasoning blocks, and tool-call scaffolding.
2. New service method `Service.FinalResponse(sessionID) (string, error)` that reads from the session log and returns the normalized text.

Per-harness responsibility:
- codex: strip `{"type":"item.completed"}` envelopes, concatenate `content` fields.
- claude: last message.content in the assistant turn.
- gemini / opencode / pi: per their respective stream shapes.

DDx then parses verdict text from FinalText, not from raw frames. No consumer-side per-harness stream knowledge.

## Boundary argument

Agent owns harness dispatch and stream format (CONTRACT-003). Anything consumer-side that does `if response.startsWith('{"type":"item.')` is patching around agent ownership. This fix moves the stream-format concern back behind the service boundary where it belongs.

## Out of scope

- Tool-call output handling — separate concern.
- Context-overflow handling inside the native loop — DDx treats that as non-terminal; if agent's native loop emits context-overflow as a recoverable final response shape, consumers will see it via FinalText naturally.
notes: Consolidated plan order 01. This is the first implementation bead because downstream typed-drain APIs need a stable normalized final text field to expose. Current code evidence: internal/harnesses/* stream aggregators already compute FinalText, but harnesses.FinalData does not expose it and service.Execute native final only emits text_delta. Keep scope tight: add a public final_text field or equivalent public final-response method, thread native result.Output and subprocess aggregate FinalText into it, update CONTRACT-003, and add a DDx-style verdict parser regression that reads only normalized final text.
title: service: expose normalized final-response text per harness — consumers shouldn't parse raw stream frames

description:
Initial paired-design comparative benchmark establishing agent-vs-pi performance baseline on a shared backing model. Produces the first datum in the agent-vs-pi parity tracking initiative (recurring measurement filed as agent-tbd-parity-tracking).

## Methodology

**Paired design.** Each selected task runs on BOTH harnesses with the same backing. Pairing controls for task-difficulty variance.

**Backing model.** openrouter qwen/qwen3.6-plus. This is the closest analog to the user's stated 'omlx qwen3.6' intent that pi --list-models actually exposes (omlx isn't in this pi config).

**Manifest arms.** Add to scripts/beadbench/manifest-v1.json:
- agent-openrouter-qwen36plus (harness=agent, provider=openrouter, model=qwen/qwen3.6-plus)
- pi-openrouter-qwen36plus (harness=pi, provider=openrouter, model=qwen/qwen3.6-plus)

**Reviewer pin.** Both arms use --review-harness codex --review-model gpt-5.5. Reviewer must NOT be claude (one of the candidates indirectly tested via pi → anthropic backing).

**Sample size.** Minimum N=8 paired tasks; target N=12 if budget permits.

**Task selection.** Use existing beadbench manifest tasks filtered to those that don't depend on prior bead state.

**Win condition (primary).** Success rate. Win = ≥10 percentage-point delta. <10 = tie.
**Tiebreaker (secondary).** Mean cost per successful close. Lower = winner.
**Tie default.** If both metrics within 5%, declare tie and default to native agent (lower indirection).

## Deliverable

docs/research/AR-2026-04-26-agent-vs-pi-qwen36plus.md containing:
1. Methodology section (paired design, N, reviewer pin, win condition).
2. Per-task pairwise outcome table: task | agent_status | pi_status | agent_cost | pi_cost | agent_duration | pi_duration | reviewer_verdict_a | reviewer_verdict_p.
3. Aggregate metrics (success rates, mean cost, mean duration, reviewer verdict counts).
4. Explicit 'winner: agent | pi | tie' line with the metric that decided.
5. Captured 'pi --list-models' output snapshot at run-time (config evidence).

## Why qwen3.6-plus and not omlx qwen3.6

The user requested omlx qwen3.6 originally but pi --list-models on the test machine shows pi is configured for [anthropic, google-gemini-cli, openai-codex, openrouter] — no omlx. Per the user's directive 'just consume what pi tells us, don't try to parse the pi config,' we pick a shared backing both harnesses can drive. openrouter qwen/qwen3.6-plus is in pi's list and reachable via our native openrouter provider, making it the closest apples-to-apples comparison.

## Out of scope

- Pi → ListModels enumeration (deferred to post-benchmark).
- Routing engine pi candidate expansion (deferred to post-benchmark).
- Reasoning_levels / reasoning_control / reasoning_wire catalog fields (placeholder agent-tbd-catalog).
- Provider wire enforcement of reasoning_wire (placeholder agent-tbd-wire).
- Reasoning=auto resolution before gate (placeholder agent-tbd-auto).
notes: Superseded by reframing on 2026-04-26. User correction: the actual benchmark target is omlx qwen3.6 on vidar (the local omlx host), NOT openrouter qwen3.6-plus. The openrouter framing was based on my misreading of the 'consume what pi tells us' directive — pi may be configurable for omlx (or already is on a different machine), and the user's stated goal is 'accomplish useful work using omlx qwen on vidar' with pi as the baseline our native agent must match. New benchmark filed as a superseding bead with the corrected backing + iteration-loop framing.
title: benchmark: agent vs pi parity on openrouter qwen3.6-plus (initial measurement)

description:
Implement real Gemini interactive quota tracking before Gemini can be promoted to automatic routing.

Context:
- A real PTY session against installed Gemini CLI 0.38.2 showed /stats model only reports model/session usage after an API call. It did not show quota remaining/limit/reset for the active concrete model.
- The same PTY session showed /model manage is the quota surface. Observed output included: Flash 4% used, reset 9:13 PM (23h 46m); Flash Lite 0% used, reset 9:27 PM (24h); Pro 100% used.
- Upstream Gemini CLI docs say /stats model shows token counts and quota information, but the installed CLI renders quota in the model management dialog. Treat both /stats model and /model manage as version-sensitive PTY surfaces.
- Auth/account freshness is not quota. Do not promote Gemini to AutoRoutingEligible=true until parsed quota evidence includes remaining/limit or used percent plus reset time by model/tier, with freshness and source recorded.

In-scope files:
- internal/harnesses/gemini/ or internal/harnesses/ptyquota/ for the PTY driver/parser/cassette fixture.
- service.go and service_capabilities.go for surfacing Gemini quota state and routing eligibility.
- service_status_test.go, service_route_attempts_test.go, service_test.go, and relevant Gemini/PTY tests.
- docs/helix/02-design/primary-harness-capability-baseline.md and docs/helix/02-design/harness-golden-integration.md for evidence updates.

Out-of-scope:
- Do not treat GOOGLE_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, OAuth files, or account tier as quota.

Second, verifier timeouts currently bubble to the outer execute timeout handler, which can label a successful execute as a run-level timeout while leaving verification marked skipped.

Out-of-scope:
- Provider reasoning wire formats.
- Pi local-provider configuration.

description:
Implements the v1 slice of ADR-007: sampling profiles live in the model catalog, resolve through a per-field merge chain, and compose with reasoning state at the provider seam. Direct response to the 2026-04-27 harness-parity failure mode where Qwen3.6 on oMLX greedy-decoded into deterministic tool-call loops.

Governing artifact: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-007-sampling-profiles-in-catalog.md

## Scope (v1)

1. **Manifest schema.** Add `sampling_profiles: map[string]config.SamplingProfile` at top level of internal/modelcatalog/manifest.go (next to but distinct from the existing `Profiles` routing field — name was reviewed and `sampling_profiles` chosen over `decoding_profiles` to avoid collision with manifest.Profiles routing concept).

2. **ModelEntry field.** Add `sampling_control string` to ModelEntry. Values `client_settable` (default), `harness_pinned`. Reserve `partial` for follow-up; do NOT implement honored-field clipping in v1.

3. **Resolver.** New package `internal/sampling` with pure function:
   `Resolve(catalog *modelcatalog.Catalog, modelID string, profileName string, providerOverride *config.SamplingProfile) (config.SamplingProfile, []SourceLog)`
   - Per-field merge (NOT bundle replace). Provider override stomps catalog value field-by-field; nil at all layers means leave-unset.
   - Returns zero-value profile when ModelEntry.sampling_control is `harness_pinned`.
   - SourceLog enumerates per-field origin for telemetry.
   - Table-driven tests covering: empty catalog + nil override → all-nil; catalog only; override only; per-field stomping; harness_pinned short-circuit.

4. **CLI plumb.** Replace cmd/agent/main.go:241-258 unpacking with a single call to internal/sampling.Resolve, passing catalog handle, resolved model ID, hardcoded profileName="code", and pc.Sampling. Wrapped harnesses (selection.Harness != "agent") MUST short-circuit to zero-profile before the resolver call.

5. **Reasoning × sampling composition at provider seam.** internal/provider/openai/openai.go:227 (compatRequestOptions) becomes the single owner of final wire-field composition. When reasoning policy resolves to non-off (model is in thinking mode), provider applies clipping/substitution per Qwen3 model-card guidance. Test: pin a Qwen3.x thinking-mode wire body and assert the final sampler fields match the documented thinking-mode bundle, NOT the raw catalog code-profile bundle.

6. **Telemetry.** Add `SamplingSource string` field to LLMRequestData (internal/session/event.go:31). Populated from resolver SourceLog: comma-separated values from {catalog, provider_config}. Empty when all fields nil.

7. **Seed catalog.** internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml gains:
   ```yaml
   sampling_profiles:
     code:
       temperature: 0.6
       top_p: 0.95
       top_k: 20
       repetition_penalty: 1.05
   ```
   Verify exact thinking-mode composition values from Qwen3 docs before adding any thinking-mode override at the provider seam — do NOT guess.

## Out of scope (per ADR-007)

- CLI sampler flags (--temperature, etc.)
- Per-(model × profile) overrides on ModelEntry
- Per-request profile selection (v1 hardcodes "code")
- `partial` sampling_control enforcement
- Range validation
- seed field plumbing (empirically ignored by oMLX 2026-04-27)

## Verification

- `go test ./internal/sampling/... ./internal/provider/openai/... ./internal/modelcatalog/...` passes.
- Manual probe: re-run /tmp/probe_samplers.py against vidar:1235 and grendel:1235 with the agent CLI driving a non-trivial prompt; confirm wire body carries the seeded code-profile fields.
- Re-run beadbench harness-parity slice (manifest arms agent-omlx-vidar-qwen36-27b-8bit and grendel equivalent) and verify the loop-detector abort no longer fires on the preflight task. Append a parity row when methodology agent-f43d1ed2 lands.
notes:
ADR-007 v1 slice landed across 6 commits (slices 1/6 through 6/6) on master:
- ec41a51: doc-stack alignment (CONTRACT-003 + FEAT-003 + ADR-007 corrections)
- 6de23a9: ServiceExecuteRequest pointer-typed Temperature/Seed
- dfaef6f: manifest schema (sampling_profiles + sampling_control)
- (slice 4): internal/sampling.Resolve resolver with table-driven tests
- 294c68a: CLI wiring + provider-seam doc + sampling_source telemetry
- 9a8d7fe: seed embedded sampling_profiles.code; ADR-007 Accepted

All 10 acceptance criteria met. Manual probe against vidar:1235
Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit confirmed wire emission of T=0.6/top_p=0.95/top_k=20
with sampling_source="catalog" on llm.request.

Caveat surfaced during probe: the user's external ~/.config/agent/models.yaml
takes precedence over the embedded manifest. Users on existing installs need
to add sampling_profiles.code to their external manifest to pick up the v1
defaults. Not a v1 blocker; consider a one-shot manifest sync helper if
this catches more than a couple users.

Follow-ups (deferred per ADR-007 Out of scope):
- Per-(model × profile) overrides on ModelEntry
- CLI sampler flags
- Per-request profile selection (replace the v1 hardcoded "code")
- partial sampling_control enforcement (Anthropic field-list clipping)
- presence_penalty plumbing if a non-thinking Qwen3.x profile is added
title: sampling: catalog sampling_profiles + sampling_control + provider-seam composition (ADR-007 v1 slice)

acceptance: 1. CONTRACT-003 amended with the three additions above and the test-contract additions. 2. ExecuteRequest, RouteRequest, ServiceRoutingActual updated in Go types. 3. Normalization validators + typed errors implemented. 4. Echo paths added (RoutingDecision, Final, session-log header). 5. Routing Policy Test Contract tests added per the minimum statements. 6. Tagged release published (e.g., v0.10.0 or appropriate version). 7. ddx repo can import the tagged release and access the new fields without errors.
description:
DDx (downstream) needs three contract additions to express retry/escalation and reviewer-pairing intent without becoming a router itself. The additions are pure observability + one return field; whether and how Fizeau evolves role/correlation-aware routing intelligence over time is a Fizeau-internal decision and is NOT specified here.

## Background

DDx today imports github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau (CONTRACT-003 at docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md). To implement Story 10's automatic escalation + reviewer pairing without DDx becoming a router (DDx must not pin Provider/Model — the spec calls that out as anti-pattern at line 374), DDx needs:

1. To know the **power** of the model that was actually dispatched, so it can request 'next call must be at least one rung stronger' via MinPower without importing catalog code into DDx.
2. To **tag** calls with what kind of work they're doing (Role) so Fizeau, telemetry consumers, and session log readers can distinguish implementer / reviewer / decomposer / etc.
3. To **link** calls that share work context (CorrelationID — e.g., 'bead_123:attempt_4') so reviewer + implementer + retry attempts can be joined in logs and aggregations.

Day 1 these are PURE observability — Fizeau's routing logic is unchanged. Future Fizeau evolution may use Role/CorrelationID for routing decisions; that's separate work, not specified by this contract change.

## What changes

ExecuteRequest gains:
- Role string — normalized: lowercased, alphanumeric+hyphen only, max 64 chars; reject invalid pre-dispatch with typed RoleNormalizationError. Empty = unset.
- CorrelationID string — normalized: printable ASCII (no control chars, no whitespace except hyphen/colon/underscore), max 256 chars; reject invalid with typed CorrelationIDNormalizationError. Empty = unset.

RouteRequest gains the same two fields with the same normalization (parity with Execute so ResolveRoute previews don't diverge).

ServiceRoutingActual gains:
- Power int — catalog-projected power value (per § Catalog Power Projection at line 983) for the actually-dispatched Model. 0 means unknown/exact-pin-only when applicable.

CONTRACT-003 reserved-cross-tool metadata keys list (today: produces_artifact, media_type) extends with:
- role
- correlation_id
Collision behavior: top-level field wins; emit MetadataKeyCollision warning event when caller also sets Metadata['role'] or Metadata['correlation_id']. Future versions may reject duplicates.

Echo behavior: Role + CorrelationID echoed into Final + RoutingDecision event.Metadata, plus session log header (one line per session). NOT echoed into per-event text_delta metadata to avoid per-token bloat (existing Metadata echo path on text_delta still applies for caller-supplied metadata that is NOT these reserved keys).

Selection precedence (line 374) is UNCHANGED for Day 1. Role and CorrelationID are observational only; they do NOT enter the precedence chain. If a future change adds routing-affecting behavior keyed on these fields, the precedence section must be amended at that time.

Routing Policy Test Contract (line 1448) gains minimum statements:
- Role + CorrelationID are echoed into RoutingDecision + Final events
- Role + CorrelationID are echoed into the session-log header
- Role + CorrelationID are NOT echoed into text_delta event metadata
- Role + CorrelationID never affect eligibility filtering Day 1
- Without a CorrelationID, routing is unchanged from baseline
- ResolveRoute and Execute observe identical routing policy for the same correlation-aware request (Day 1: both ignore for routing)
- Invalid Role / CorrelationID values rejected pre-dispatch with the typed errors
- ServiceRoutingActual.Power reflects the catalog-projected power of the dispatched Model
- When caller sets both top-level Role and Metadata['role'], top-level wins and a MetadataKeyCollision warning is emitted

## What this enables downstream (informational)

DDx will use these fields to:
- Compute next-attempt MinPower from previous.RoutingActual.Power + rung_step (cost-tier escalation)
- Tag implementer + reviewer calls with Role for telemetry; share CorrelationID for trace-join
- Set MinPower = implementer.RoutingActual.Power + 1 on the reviewer call (best-effort 'more powerful'); 'different provider' is best-effort emergent, not promised
- When same-provider results on review, log a degradation event for operator visibility

DDx will NOT:
- Pin Provider/Model
- Implement any tiebreaker keyed on CorrelationID
- Define role-specific routing policy

## Out of scope

- Role-specific Fizeau routing policy (config registry, per-role defaults, etc.)
- Correlation-aware routing intelligence (tiebreakers, diversity bias, learned preferences)
- Multi-tenant correlation namespacing
- Cancellation semantics for any future correlation state
- DDx→Fizeau outcome-feedback API
- Anything keyed on Role or CorrelationID that affects routing

If Fizeau evolves any of the above, those are separate proposals with their own design docs (correlation-aware routing must specify state lifecycle, multi-tenant boundaries, cancellation behavior, ResolveRoute parity, and full test contract — none of that is in scope here).
title: CONTRACT-003: add Role + CorrelationID + Power for downstream observability and DDx-side retry/escalation

description:
Bead C of the prompt-caching plan (codex-refined). Wire native Anthropic provider to use the Anthropic SDK's cache_control feature so multi-turn sessions hit Anthropic's prompt cache (10x discount on cache reads). Depends on bead A (agent-cccc2df7) for the CachePolicy opt-out.

Anthropic uses the official Go SDK at github.com/anthropics/anthropic-sdk-go. Cache breakpoints are set via the SDK's typed CacheControl field on ant.TextBlockParam (system blocks) and ant.ToolUnionParam (tool definitions). NOT raw JSON.

Concrete edits:

1. Refactor internal/provider/anthropic/anthropic.go: extract a shared buildSystemBlocks(msgs []agent.Message, opts agent.Options) []ant.TextBlockParam helper consumed by both Chat (line 56) and ChatStream (line 208). System block construction is currently duplicated inline in both functions. (convertTools at line 190 is already shared.)

2. In convertTools: set CacheControl: ant.NewCacheControlEphemeralParam() on the LAST tool in the returned slice. Earlier tools have no CacheControl. (Anthropic's policy: a breakpoint marks the END of a cacheable prefix; placing it on the last item caches everything up to and including that item.)

3. In buildSystemBlocks: same shape — set CacheControl on the LAST TextBlockParam in the returned slice.

4. Honor CachePolicy="off" from bead A: when set, skip BOTH cache_control markers entirely. Default and empty CachePolicy: emit both.

5. Drop the original plan's optional 3rd breakpoint on "stable message context" — too vague to define without scope creep.

6. Add a Caching section to docs/helix/02-design/architecture.md describing:
   - Prefix order invariant: tools → system → conversation history → trailing user message
   - The two-marker placement (last tool, last system) and why
   - Compaction caveat: internal/compaction rewrites retained messages; a compaction event is expected to break the cache prefix and reset cost. This is intentional, not a bug.
   - Tool-mutation caveat: dynamic tool descriptions (any text in Tool.Description that varies per turn) silently kill caching. Tools registered via agent.ToolDef should be deterministic.

7. Honor CachePolicy="off" cost semantics: when off, the loop's cost attribution (bead D, agent-XXX) emits CacheReadAmount/CacheWriteAmount as explicit *float64(0.0), NOT nil. (Coordinated with bead D.)

In-scope files:
- internal/provider/anthropic/anthropic.go (refactor + cache_control wiring)
- internal/provider/anthropic/anthropic_test.go (typed-param assertions)
- internal/provider/anthropic/anthropic_wire_test.go (NEW: httptest wire-body assertion)
- docs/helix/02-design/architecture.md (Caching section)

Out of scope:
- Cost-attribution wiring (bead D)
- OpenAI/openai-compat caching (gemini, lmstudio, omlx, openrouter all use openai-compat; their cache discount is via OpenAI auto-caching only — covered by bead B's regression gate)
- Subprocess harness caching (claude code, codex, gemini, opencode, pi all manage their own caching)

`go test ./... -count=1` cannot complete locally for the same sandbox networking reason across multiple httptest-based packages, including `service_http_provider_test.go`, `cmd/agent/update_test.go`, and `internal/provider/anthropic/conformance_test.go`.

acceptance: (1) GET /v1/models is enumerated by the discovery layer; the catalog is the canonical model-ID source per provider. (2) Bare model names ('qwen3-coder-next') passed by callers are normalized to canonical IDs ('qwen/qwen3-coder-next') by suffix-match against the catalog, with explicit ambiguity errors. (3) providers command shows http-connected and inference-ready-models per provider. (4) Routing policy consumes inference-ready-models, not 'connected (N models)'. (5) Regression test uses a mock LM Studio that lists 'qwen/foo' but rejects 'foo' on chat/completions; discovery normalizes 'foo' → 'qwen/foo' and the probe succeeds.
description:
Discovered 2026-04-15 while routing work to vidar-coder (LM Studio on vidar:1234).

## Two-part bug

### Part 1: bare-name JIT miss (the actual root cause)

Initial probe: `POST /v1/chat/completions {model: 'qwen3-coder-next', ...}` → 400 'No models loaded'.

We assumed LM Studio had no model loaded. Wrong. The actual model is listed by /v1/models as 'qwen/qwen3-coder-next' (with the 'qwen/' prefix). LM Studio's just-in-time loading IS enabled and DOES auto-load models on demand — but only when the requested model ID matches one in /v1/models exactly. Bare-name 'qwen3-coder-next' didn't match, so JIT couldn't fire, and LM Studio returned the misleading 'No models loaded' error instead of 'unknown model'.

Verified by re-probing with 'qwen/qwen3-coder-next' — JIT fired, model loaded, responded 'OK' in ~60s on cold start.

### Part 2: discovery should be standard practice

The routing/probe layer should never trust user-supplied bare model names against an LM Studio provider. The standard discovery flow should be:

1. GET /v1/models → enumerate the catalog the server is offering
2. For each catalog ID, optionally probe POST /v1/chat/completions with a 1-token request and a generous timeout (60s+ on cold start, 3s on warm)
3. Cache the (provider, model_id) → inference-ready bool with a short TTL
4. When user code asks for 'model qwen3-coder-next' against this provider, normalize against the discovered catalog: if there's exactly one match by suffix, accept it; if zero or multiple, error with the candidate list

This converts the 'No models loaded' misleading error into a clear 'requested model qwen3-coder-next not in catalog; closest match: qwen/qwen3-coder-next' (or just transparent normalization).

## Impact on cost-tiered routing

The cost-tiered routing policy (DDx bead ddx-dc231144 tier-based auto-escalation) needs reliable provider readiness signals. Without discovery + normalization:
- bare-name configs silently fail and force escalation
- the 'connected (N models)' status is unreliable as a routing input
- every misrouted attempt burns latency and forces the next tier

## Fix scope

- Update routing/health probe in cmd/ddx-agent/routing.go (or equivalent) to enumerate /v1/models and probe each listed model with its exact ID
- Add a model-id normalization layer that maps bare names to catalog IDs by suffix-match (with explicit error on ambiguity)
- Cache probe results per (provider, model) for a short TTL (30s reasonable)
- Surface the distinction in 'ddx-agent providers' output: http-connected (from /v1/models) vs inference-ready-models (the subset that actually responds)
resolution: fixed: /v1/models discovery + bare-name normalization in provider/openai
title: bug: /v1/models is not a liveness probe — providers shows 'connected' when LM Studio has no model loaded

acceptance: go test ./provider/openai/... covers all 10 cases above; no new test failures
description:
AC-FEAT-003-06 and AC-FEAT-003-07 specify that limit discovery and flavor detection must be tested.

Current state: discovery_test.go covers DiscoverModels, RankModels, NormalizeModelID, and lazy model discovery but has NO tests for:
- LookupModelLimits (all dispatch paths)
- probeProviderFlavor (omlx probe wins, lmstudio probe wins, both fail, flavor param bypasses probe)
- lmstudioLimits (loaded_context_length vs max_context_length priority)
- omlxLimits (model match by EqualFold)
- openrouterLimits (hyphen-dot normalization)

Required tests (httptest.NewServer mocks):
1. LookupModelLimits: explicit flavor='lmstudio' routes without probing
2. LookupModelLimits: explicit flavor='omlx' routes without probing
3. LookupModelLimits: explicit flavor='openrouter' routes without probing
4. LookupModelLimits: ambiguous URL fires probe; omlx probe wins
5. LookupModelLimits: ambiguous URL fires probe; lmstudio probe wins
6. LookupModelLimits: both probes fail → falls back to openAIIdentity result
7. LookupModelLimits: unreachable endpoint returns ModelLimits{} without error
8. lmstudioLimits: prefers loaded_context_length over max_context_length
9. omlxLimits: case-insensitive model match
10. openrouterLimits: hyphen-dot normalization (claude-haiku-4-5 == claude-haiku-4.5)
title: test: LookupModelLimits and probeProviderFlavor unit tests

acceptance: All child beads created from the 2026-04-10 acceptance review are closed; each targeted AC ID has an automated test or explicit environment-gated integration test path, and the default Go test suite remains green.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Docs are authoritative · Acceptance criteria must be deterministic and executable · Favor targeted regression coverage over vague smoke tests</principles>
<concerns>go-std | helix contract-driven design | test traceability</concerns>
<practices>Feature specs own authoritative AC IDs · Build-tag external tests must skip explicitly when env is unavailable · CLI behavior should be asserted through built binaries or `go run` wrappers, not by scraping implementation internals</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 observability surfaces and cost attribution</adrs>
<governing>PRD acceptance sketches now defer to feature-level AC tables added on 2026-04-10 in FEAT-001 through FEAT-007. This epic exists to close the remaining test coverage gaps against those AC tables.</governing>
</context-digest>

Review completed on 2026-04-10 tightened the feature specs with stable AC IDs. The current repo has broad unit coverage, but there are still uncovered or weakly covered criteria around runtime edge cases, CLI behavior, provider failure paths, routing attribution, usage/reporting surfaces, and installer/update flows.

Create and close child test beads until every acceptance criterion introduced in the 2026-04-10 feature-spec review either has a deterministic automated test in this repo or an explicit environment-gated integration/e2e test surface with skip semantics. Keep scope to test coverage and traceability; implementation fixes should only be made when required to make the new tests pass.
title: Close acceptance-test coverage gaps against feature AC matrix

1. `internal/harnesses/codex` contains a sanitized fixture or synthetic fixture that represents the current Codex JSONL tool call and tool result shape.
2. Codex stream parser tests prove tool call IDs, tool names, JSON inputs, outputs/errors, ordering, and final text/usage are preserved.
3. `Service.Execute` golden replay for the codex harness includes tool-event evidence and passes without live credentials.
4. If Codex tool events are now supported, `service_capabilities.go`, `service_test.go`, and CONTRACT-003 promote codex `ToolEvents` from unsupported to optional with a clear detail string.

description:
Codex is a primary harness and should be as close as practical to Claude and the native agent harness. Today `internal/harnesses/codex/stream.go` only maps text and usage events from `codex exec --json`; all other event types are skipped, so CONTRACT-003 `tool_call` and `tool_result` events are unsupported while Claude and native agent expose them.

Implement Codex JSONL tool-event parsing from current Codex CLI output. If the exact live schema is uncertain, first capture a sanitized fixture from a Codex run that performs a tool call, then implement the parser against that fixture. Keep opencode/pi/gemini tool parity out of scope for this bead.
title: harness: map codex tool events to CONTRACT-003

acceptance:
1. Before starting, confirm no unrelated `bench matrix`, `harbor run`, `terminal-bench`, `vidar`, or `omlx` benchmark process is contending for the same Vidar model server.
2. `git submodule update --init scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2` succeeds at the pinned terminal-bench-2 commit.
3. Run the full 267-cell command from the description with `OMLX_API_KEY=local`, `HARBOR_AGENT_ARTIFACT=$(pwd)/fiz-linux-amd64`, profile vidar-qwen3-6-27b, harness ddx-agent, reps=3, subset scripts/beadbench/external/termbench-full.json, tasks-dir scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2, and jobs=1.
4. `go run ./cmd/bench matrix-aggregate <matrix-dir>` passes.
5. `<matrix-dir>/matrix.json` contains at least 27 entries with `final_status == "graded_pass"`.
6. Commit the matrix artifacts, including matrix.json and matrix.md, plus a memo under `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` or docs/research/ that records the command, environment prerequisites, total graded_pass count, and comparison note that the superseded prior gate has no committed PIVOT+PRESET artifact.
7. Do not close this bead from a partial matrix, a concurrently contaminated run, or a run below 27 graded_pass.
description:
Manual benchmark task: re-run the full 267-cell TB-2 Vidar matrix after PLAN-3 closes. PLAN added a pre-loop task decomposition step targeting reasoning stalls and missing-output failures. The minimum threshold remains 27/267 graded_pass (10%). The earlier phrase "exceed prior gate result by >=5pp" is historical context only: dependency agent-b2ce8974 was closed WONTFIX/superseded and does not provide a committed PIVOT+PRESET gate artifact to compare against.

This bead is not suitable for normal execute-loop work. Prior attempts showed one valid cell took 827.001s and produced graded_fail, and running while another Vidar/TB-2 benchmark is active contaminates the measurement. Run only in a dedicated long-running benchmark window with exclusive access to the Vidar omlx model server.

Required setup:
- Wait for or stop unrelated Vidar/TB-2 benchmark jobs.
- `git submodule update --init scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2`
- Ensure scripts/beadbench/external/termbench-full.json contains the 89-task TB-2 subset at dataset commit 53ff2b87d621bdb97b455671f2bd9728b7d86c11.
- Ensure scripts/benchmark/profiles/vidar-qwen3-6-27b.yaml points at the intended local Vidar endpoint.

Command:
OMLX_API_KEY=local HARBOR_AGENT_ARTIFACT=$(pwd)/fiz-linux-amd64 go run ./cmd/bench matrix --profiles=vidar-qwen3-6-27b --harnesses=ddx-agent --reps=3 --subset=scripts/beadbench/external/termbench-full.json --tasks-dir=scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2 --out=benchmark-results/matrix-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ) --jobs=1

After completion, run:
go run ./cmd/bench matrix-aggregate <matrix-dir>

Commit the result artifacts and a memo only if the matrix completes and contains at least 27 graded_pass results.
notes: SUPERSEDED 2026-05-07: Operator decision is to skip the stale TB-2.0 matrix path and migrate benchmarks to TerminalBench 2.1 before running a full sweep. Do not run this TB-2.0 pinned 267-cell Vidar matrix as a current gate. Replacement work: fizeau-ee77ec7f (migrate runners to TerminalBench 2.1) and fizeau-c770193a (run full TerminalBench 2.1 sweep). Historical TB-2.0 details remain useful only as context for prior local benchmark attempts.
title: BENCH: Re-run TB-2 after PLAN; require ≥10% pass rate

2. N>=8 paired tasks executed; doc states N and any task-skip reasons.
3. Top-3 gaps section names a concrete lever (compaction/tools/system prompt/reasoning) for each of the top 3 ranked observations, with task-level evidence (e.g. specific tasks where the gap manifested).
4. First row appended to docs/research/agent-vs-pi-parity.md (per agent-f43d1ed2's tracker — that bead still requires that file exist so this bead also creates the file if missing).

description:
Child 3 of agent-b5d67344's iteration-loop split. Execute the actual paired benchmark and append the data sections to the deliverable doc. Long-running operator-driven work (3-8 hours wall-clock); not suitable for a single execute-bead pass.

Depends on agent-bea748e7 (manifest arms) and agent-1bffdd79 (doc skeleton).

## How to run

  python scripts/beadbench/run_beadbench.py     --arms agent-omlx-vidar-qwen36,pi-omlx-vidar-qwen36     --tasks <curated-8-task-subset>     --pair --output docs/research/AR-2026-04-26-agent-vs-pi-omlx-vidar-qwen36/

(exact CLI subject to run_beadbench.py's actual flags; check --help)

## Deliverable updates

After the run completes:

### 3. Per-task pairwise outcome table

| task | agent_status | pi_status | agent_cost | pi_cost | agent_duration | pi_duration | agent_review | pi_review |
| ...

### 4. Aggregate metrics

- agent success rate: X%, pi success rate: Y%, delta: Δ pp
- mean cost ratio (agent/pi): R
- mean duration ratio (agent/pi): D
- reviewer-verdict distribution per arm
- winner: agent | pi | tie (with deciding metric)

### 5. Top-3 gaps

Ranked by which lever (compaction / tools / system prompt / reasoning) the iteration should attack first. Each gap with concrete evidence:
- 'agent failed N tasks via tool-call loops; pi did not — tools area is top gap'
- 'agent's mean cost was 2.3x pi's at same success rate — system prompt verbosity / preset choice'
- etc.

These gaps drive the iteration beads (child 4, filed reactively).

## Why this isn't an agent execute-bead pass

Each Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit task runs 5-30+ minutes including verifier (cargo test / go test). 16 paired runs is 3-8 hours wall-clock plus per-task sandbox setup with multi-GB repo clones. Single execute-bead Claude pass cannot keep an interactive session alive for that duration without timing out. Run as long-running background or operator-driven; the closing bead just needs to land the populated doc.
title: harness-parity: run the benchmark and append per-task results + winner + top-3 gaps

description:
Gemini should become a primary automatic routing target only if the harness reaches the same operational bar as primary targets. Today it has a basic subprocess runner and fake-output usage parser tests, but no authenticated live evidence, model discovery, reasoning control, quota/account status, or catalog/profile mapping that prevents empty-model smart routes.

Promotion requirements:
- discover supported Gemini models from the CLI/API surface and map catalog/profile refs to concrete Gemini model IDs
- implement and test any Gemini reasoning/thinking controls if the CLI supports them; otherwise document unsupported status in the capability matrix

- only after the above, change routing metadata so Gemini is no longer experimental and can compete in automatic smart-tier routing

In-scope files are expected to include internal/harnesses/gemini/, internal/harnesses/registry.go, service_capabilities.go, service_routing.go or internal/routing tests, and docs/helix/02-design/harness-golden-integration.md.

acceptance:
1. scripts/beadbench/corpus.yaml exists with a documented schema (version 1) and a JSON-Schema-style validator that rejects entries missing bead_id / capability / promoted / promoted_by.
2. scripts/beadbench/corpus/<bead-id>.yaml per-bead detail files use the documented schema; loader rejects files referencing a bead_id not in the index.
3. ddx bead promote <id> (or equivalent slash command) gates on operator confirmation, writes both files atomically, refuses to promote already-corpus or open beads.
4. scripts/beadbench/corpus/capabilities.yaml lists at least 5 seeded capability tags with descriptions.
5. The corpus has at least 3 promoted beads at landing time (initial migration pass).
6. Manifest filter: scripts/beadbench supports `--corpus-only` and `--capability=<tag>` filters that constrain task set to the corpus.
7. Documentation: docs/helix/02-design/benchmark-corpus.md (or equivalent) describes the curation rules and promotion criteria.
description:
Establishes the curated 'capability/failure-mode corpus' that drives our internal benchmarking — distinct from external benchmarks (SWE-bench, TerminalBench, etc.) which compare us against the field. The corpus answers 'are WE getting better' across model, harness, and provider revisions over time.

Direct response to the user's signal: 'as we identify "interesting" bead executions, we should start tracking them so we can make a benchmark out of them. Each should represent some particular capability or failure, which we can then use to track and compare across model, harness, and provider revisions.'

## Scope

### 1. Corpus index + per-bead schema

scripts/beadbench/corpus.yaml — the canonical index:

```yaml
version: 1
beads:
  - id: agent-39f79181
    capability: sampling-resolver-cli-wiring
    promoted: 2026-04-27
    promoted_by: erik
  - id: agent-b6b15fb0
    failure_mode: openai-flavor-thinking-leak
    promoted: 2026-04-26
    promoted_by: erik
```

scripts/beadbench/corpus/<bead-id>.yaml — per-bead detail:

```yaml
bead_id: agent-39f79181
project_root: /Users/erik/Projects/agent
base_rev: ec41a51        # before the change
known_good_rev: 9a8d7fe  # the resolution
captured: 2026-04-27
capability: sampling-resolver-cli-wiring
failure_mode: ''         # populated when promoted BECAUSE of failure
difficulty: easy|medium|hard
prompt_kind: implement-with-spec | port-by-analogy | debug-and-fix | review
notes: free text, what makes this bead instructive
```

Each entry must reference a real closed bead in .ddx/beads.jsonl with reachable base_rev / known_good_rev. Validator at load time.

### 2. /promote-bead curation flow

Manual gate — automated promotion would dilute the corpus.

Slash command (or `ddx bead promote <id>`): after an execution finishes, prompt the operator with:
  - 'this bead surfaced X. Promote to corpus? [y/N]'
  - capability tag (free-form, validated against known tags + warning when introducing a new one)
  - failure_mode tag (free-form, only when the bead surfaced a failure)
  - difficulty
  - notes (one paragraph)

Writes both the corpus.yaml entry and the per-bead detail file. Refuses to promote if the bead is open, has no closing commit, or already exists in the corpus.

### 3. Initial migration

Pass through the last ~20 closed beads on master. Manually identify 3-5 genuinely instructive ones — examples of clear capability demonstrations or notable failure modes. Promote with capability tags. Goal: corpus is non-empty when this lands so the schema is exercised, not aspirational.

Candidates from recent history (illustrative, not authoritative — the migration pass picks them):
- agent-39f79181 (ADR-007 v1 sampling resolver wiring)
- agent-b6b15fb0 (provider-wire thinking-field leak fix)
- agent-37aeb88e (the 2026-04-27 omlx Qwen tool-loop preflight failure)

### 4. Capability taxonomy seed

scripts/beadbench/corpus/capabilities.yaml — controlled vocabulary, additive:

```yaml
- name: sampling-resolver-cli-wiring
  area: cli
  description: ...
- name: openai-flavor-thinking-leak
  area: provider
  description: ...
```

Bead promotions reference these by name. Adding a new capability requires updating this file (= explicit decision, not free-form drift).

### 5. Run-time integration

Manifest arms in scripts/beadbench/manifest-v1.json gain a corpus filter — 'run only beads tagged with capability X' or 'run all corpus beads'. The existing bench runner already accepts task IDs; this just constrains the task set.

## Out of scope (separate beads)

- TerminalBench / SWE-bench / other external-benchmark adapters (sister bead).
- Benchmark-history aggregator (joining runs into a capability × model × harness × provider × revision matrix).
- Per-capability regression detection (alerts when a previously-passing capability fails).

## Why epic

This is curation infrastructure that compounds — every promotion adds value but no single promotion is the whole feature. The schema needs to land, then the slash command, then the migration pass, then the runner integration. Filing as epic so the children can be sequenced clearly.
notes: Landed in PR #3 (squash-merged commit d539c7f) on 2026-04-28. v1 implementation by subagent: corpus.yaml index + per-bead detail files + atomic Promote with rollback + ddx-agent corpus promote/validate/list subcommands + run_beadbench.py --corpus-only / --capability filters + 7-tag controlled vocabulary + 3 seeded beads + benchmark-corpus.md docs. All acceptance gates met. PR merged via admin (CI flake on pre-existing route-status tests, filed agent-901cd778).
title: benchmark: internal corpus.yaml schema + /promote-bead curation flow

acceptance:
1. New service instances and normal status/routing calls trigger best-effort asynchronous Claude/Codex quota refresh when cache is missing or stale, with throttle/cooldown so calls do not spawn repeated PTY probes.
2. ResolveRoute remains cache-only in the foreground: it does not block on live probes, and stale/missing cache still makes the primary harness ineligible until refresh succeeds.
3. Tests prove refresh is initiated automatically without explicit HealthCheck, and that repeated calls within the margin do not repeatedly probe.

description:
Claude and Codex automatic routing correctly consumes durable quota caches, but cache refresh is currently owned only by explicit HealthCheck calls. That leaves humans or external polling responsible for keeping caches fresh. Move ownership into the service: normal service lifecycle/activity should trigger throttled asynchronous refresh for primary subscription harnesses while foreground routing continues reading only durable cache state.

In-scope files: service.go, service_providers.go, service_routing.go, service_status/list harness tests, service provider tests.

Out-of-scope: synchronous live probes in ResolveRoute or promoting secondary harnesses.
notes: Implemented service-owned primary quota refresh. New services start a best-effort Claude/Codex refresh for missing/stale/incomplete caches and wait only the bounded startup window; ListHarnesses, RouteStatus, and ResolveRoute trigger debounced async refresh without blocking foreground routing; optional QuotaRefreshInterval enables server timer refresh. Added tests for automatic/throttled refresh, startup wait/timeout continuation, timer refresh, and ResolveRoute nonblocking behavior. Verified go test ./... passes.
title: service: automate primary harness quota cache refresh

acceptance:
1. ~/Projects/ddx/cli/go.mod requires github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent at >= v0.9.20.
2. ddx binary at ~/.local/bin/ddx (rebuilt) accepts a config containing 'type: lucebox' and 'type: vllm' provider entries without erroring at load.
3. 'ddx agent execute-bead <bead> --provider lucebox --model Qwen3.6-27B-Q4_K_M --harness agent --dry-run' succeeds without unknown-type errors.

description:
~/Projects/ddx/cli/go.mod pins github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.16. Lucebox (v0.9.18) and vllm (v0.9.19) provider types were added after that pin. Result: beadbench shells out to /home/erik/.local/bin/ddx (which embeds the older agent module) and refuses to load any user config containing a lucebox or vllm provider entry, with the error:

  config: provider "lucebox" has unknown type "lucebox" (use openai, openrouter, lmstudio, omlx, ollama, or anthropic)

This blocks beadbench arms for those provider types. Today's tier-3 sweep (2026-04-27) had to strip lucebox + vllm from the user config to run the available arms (vidar-omlx, bragi-lmstudio, openrouter).

## Scope

1. ~/Projects/ddx/cli/go.mod: bump 'github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent' from v0.9.16 to v0.9.20 (or current latest agent release at the time this lands).
2. go mod tidy in cli/.
3. make build install (or whatever DDX uses) so ~/.local/bin/ddx picks up the new module.
4. Verify: 'ddx agent execute-bead --provider lucebox --model Qwen3.6-27B-Q4_K_M --harness agent <bead>' loads the config without error.
5. Re-add the agent-bragi-lucebox-qwen36 arm to scripts/beadbench/manifest-v1.json (already added in the agent repo; just confirm DDX accepts it).

## Out of scope

- Anything that would require API breakage in the agent module (we should be picking up additive changes only).
- Lucebox tool-choice gap (separate upstream concern, see docs/research/lucebox-tool-support-2026-04-27.md).

## Why P2

Doesn't block ordinary beadbench use (vidar-omlx + bragi-lmstudio + openrouter still work). Blocks getting lucebox / vllm beadbench numbers, which is informative-but-not-critical.
notes: ddx-cli go.mod was already at agent v0.9.21 (latest). Issue was the installed binary at ~/.local/bin/ddx pre-dated the bump. Rebuilt via 'cd ~/Projects/ddx && make build' and replaced the installed binary. Verified: 'python3 scripts/beadbench/run_beadbench.py --arm agent-bragi-lucebox-qwen36 --dry-run' loads the lucebox provider config without the prior 'unknown type lucebox' error.
title: ddx-cli: bump agent dep to v0.9.20 so beadbench can drive lucebox + vllm arms

acceptance:
1. New test file internal/sdk/openaicompat/client_cache_stability_test.go exists.
2. go test ./internal/sdk/openaicompat -run 'TestOpenAIRequestPrefixIsByteStableAcrossTurns' passes: two Chat calls with identical tools+system but differing trailing user message produce HTTP bodies whose byte-content up to the differing message is identical.
3. go test ./internal/sdk/openaicompat -run 'TestOpenAIRequestPreservesToolOrder' passes: swapping tool order in []agent.ToolDef produces a different wire body in the expected position.
4. The fixture tools include at least one tool with nested object schema, a required-fields array, and per-property descriptions.
5. The httptest server captures and inspects actual HTTP request bytes — not local json.Marshal output.
6. go test ./... -count=1 passes.
description:
Bead B of the prompt-caching plan (codex-refined). Pure regression gate; no behavior change. Establishes that OpenAI auto-prefix-caching has stable input on our side: tools and system prompts must serialize byte-identically across turns when the caller's intent is identical except for trailing user messages.

Why: OpenAI auto-caches prefixes ≥1024 tokens; a single byte change anywhere in the prefix invalidates the entire cache. Today nothing asserts that internal/sdk/openaicompat/client.go produces byte-stable output. Tool params unmarshaled into map[string]interface{} at internal/sdk/openaicompat/client.go:285 — Go's json.Marshal sorts map keys, which is good, but SDK internals or option ordering could still vary.

The test must operate at the wire level (httptest), NOT at marshal-equality level. Capture HTTP request bodies from real Chat calls and assert byte-equality on the prefix.

Concrete edits:
- internal/sdk/openaicompat/client_cache_stability_test.go (new file).
- TestOpenAIRequestPrefixIsByteStableAcrossTurns: spin up httptest.NewServer; call Chat twice with identical tools+system+conversation prefix but a differing trailing user message; capture both HTTP bodies; locate the differing-message boundary in each; assert byte-equality on the prefix. Use a fixture tools array containing tools with nested JSON schema, required arrays, and descriptions (real complexity, not trivial).
- TestOpenAIRequestPreservesToolOrder: negative test. Swap two tools in []agent.ToolDef; assert the wire body differs in the expected position. Proves order-preservation matters and is preserved.

Test must be table-driven and run against the openai-compat layer used by all OpenAI-compatible providers (lmstudio, omlx, openrouter, openai, anthropic-via-openai-route if applicable).

Out of scope:
- Behavior changes to openai-compat
- Anthropic provider (separate SDK; bead C)
- The marshal-equality test pattern (codex confirmed it's a weak gate; do NOT add)

title: cache: openai-compat prefix-stability wire-level regression test

acceptance:
1. Embedding callers can express unset, auto, explicit off, low, medium, high, extended reasoning levels, and explicit numeric values through one public `Reasoning` field.
2. Public Go API includes named constants and `ReasoningTokens(n int) Reasoning`; no separate public `ReasoningBudget` field is introduced.
3. Shared leaf package parser/normalizer is used by root `agent` and is importable by internal packages without root-agent import cycles.
4. Unit test proves `Request.Reasoning` wins over legacy thinking/effort-style aliases if both are set during migration.
5. Unit test proves unset request reasoning preserves a provider-level positive thinking/reasoning budget.
6. Unit test proves request-level `reasoning=off` and `ReasoningTokens(0)` suppress a provider-level positive thinking budget.
7. Unit test proves request-level `low|medium|high` maps to expected internal numeric budgets for a numeric-only provider path.
8. Unit test proves positive numeric reasoning values are parsed as explicit numeric budgets.
9. Unit test proves extended values such as `x-high` normalize to `xhigh` and are preserved for adapters that advertise support.
10. Unit test proves explicit unsupported extended values and over-limit numeric values fail clearly rather than silently downgrading or dropping.
11. Unit test proves `omlx`/unsupported flavors do not serialize reasoning/thinking fields when unsupported.
12. Unit test or shaped HTTP assertion proves sync `Chat` and streaming `ChatStream` resolve reasoning consistently.
13. Any public embedding API rename/type break is documented in a changelog or migration note.
14. `go test ./...` passes.
description:
Add embedding-level support for explicit reasoning control without losing the ability to distinguish unset from off. Current state: `agent.Options` has `ThinkingLevel` and `ThinkingBudget int`, but `0` means unset; `internal/provider/openai` starts from provider config budget and only overrides with request budget when `opts.ThinkingBudget > 0`. Therefore an embedding caller cannot force reasoning/thinking off when provider config has a positive budget. `agent.Request` also has no reasoning field, so `loop.go` only forwards `MaxTokens` into provider options. Current harness metadata also only models low/medium/high for some harnesses, while others already expose values like minimal/max; this must become extensible.

Required behavior:
- Create a small leaf package such as `internal/reasoning` that owns the canonical Reasoning scalar, constants, `ReasoningTokens(n)` helper, parser, normalization, and parsed policy representation. Root package `agent` re-exports the public type/constants/helpers for embedding callers. Internal packages import the leaf package to avoid importing root `agent`.
- Add public request-level reasoning control as one field, preferably `Request.Reasoning Reasoning` where `type Reasoning string` is re-exported from the leaf package.
- Add named constants for public values (`ReasoningAuto`, `ReasoningOff`, `ReasoningLow`, `ReasoningMedium`, `ReasoningHigh`, `ReasoningMinimal`, `ReasoningXHigh`, `ReasoningMax`, etc.) and helper `ReasoningTokens(n int) Reasoning` for numeric values.
- Do not add a separate public `Request.ReasoningBudget` or `Options.ReasoningBudget` field. Internally, parsing may produce a `ReasoningPolicy` struct containing kind/level/tokens, but the public input is one reasoning value.
- Add normalization helpers shared across CLI/service/config/provider paths: empty -> unset, auto -> auto, none/false/0 -> off, x-high -> xhigh, positive integer -> numeric token budget, preserve provider-specific level strings that are explicitly supported.
- Parser must accept scalar string and scalar integer inputs from YAML/JSON/config/catalog layers and normalize them to one canonical reasoning value.
- During migration, if public `Request.Reasoning` and legacy `Options.ThinkingLevel`/`ThinkingBudget`-style values are both present on the same call path, `Reasoning` wins. Cleanup bead agent-f78c3f25 removes or time-bounds the legacy aliases.
- Provider implementations must map named levels to numeric budgets when the underlying API/model accepts only numeric reasoning-token limits, using provider/catalog-specific maps when available and the portable defaults from the spec only as fallback for low/medium/high.
- Provider implementations must reject explicit over-limit numeric values with a clear error.
- `Run` must forward request reasoning into provider options.
- Unset request reasoning must still honor provider-level config, including a positive configured budget.
- Explicit `reasoning=off` or numeric 0 must suppress provider-level positive thinking/reasoning budgets.
- Explicit unsupported extended values or unsupported numeric values must fail clearly at the routing/adapter boundary instead of being silently downgraded to high or dropped.
- For native OpenAI-compatible providers, map reasoning to the appropriate provider-specific knob only after provider/catalog/config resolution. For current LM Studio-compatible behavior this means mapping to the nonstandard `thinking` budget field.
- Apply consistent behavior to both `Chat` and `ChatStream`; today only streaming injects the nonstandard `thinking` field.
- Keep provider capability detection as the final serialization gate; `omlx`, `openrouter`, `openai`, and `ollama` must never receive unsupported reasoning/thinking fields.
- Record release-note risk if any public embedding field changes name or type.

In-scope files:
- agent.go
- loop.go
- internal/reasoning/*
- internal/provider/openai/openai.go
- internal/provider/openai/openai_test.go or protocol support tests
- thinking_test.go, renamed or supplemented if appropriate

Out-of-scope:
- CLI flags.
- ServiceExecuteRequest plumbing.
- Model catalog default changes.
- Anthropic extended-thinking API support unless it already exists and can be wired trivially.
title: lib: make reasoning/off tri-state in Request and provider options

description:
The initial Bragi beadbench run timed out after 20 minutes, but `scripts/beadbench/run_beadbench.py` only wrote `timeout.txt` from `subprocess.TimeoutExpired`. It did not persist partial stdout/stderr, a partially produced execute-result JSON object, or enough progress evidence to distinguish a stuck model from a slow but healthy run. Timeout evidence is especially important for local Qwen 3.6 baselines, where slow progress can be acceptable but reasoning loops are a key failure mode.

In-scope files:
- scripts/beadbench/run_beadbench.py
- scripts/beadbench/README.md

Out-of-scope:
- Provider reasoning serialization.
- Changing DDx execute-bead internals.
title: beadbench: preserve partial output on timeout

acceptance:
1. Public types `RouteCandidate` (exported struct) and `RouteDecision.Candidates []RouteCandidate` field exist with godoc.
2. `ResolveRoute` returns a populated `Candidates` slice on both success AND error. On error, `Decision` is non-nil with `Candidates` populated and `Harness/Provider/Model` empty.
3. `internal/routing.Candidate` → `RouteCandidate` mapping lives in `service_routing.go` as a pure function; unit tested.
4. Rejection reasons from `gating.go` surface unchanged in `RouteCandidate.Reason`. Test asserts a seeded rejection reason (e.g., "model not in harness allow-list" from agent-bab52778) reaches the public decision.
5. `errors.As(err, &decErr)` where `decErr` exposes the failed candidates list works for the "no viable" typed error.
6. Existing tests in `service_routing_test.go` and `service_route_attempts_test.go` pass unchanged. New tests cover: success path has candidates; error path has candidates; rejection reasons are preserved.
7. No breaking change to existing `RouteDecision` consumers — new field is purely additive. Callers ignoring it see no behavioral difference.
8. godoc example showing a caller iterating `Candidates` to log a full trace.
description:
## Motivation

`internal/routing.Resolve` already ranks candidates and attaches rejection reasons internally (`Candidate.Eligible`, `Candidate.Reason`). The public `agent.RouteDecision` (`service.go:324`) surfaces only the winning pick (`Harness, Provider, Endpoint, Model, Reason`). `service_routing.go:41` drops the full candidate list on error. DDx consumers (route-status CLI, providers UI, bead tier-attempt evidence) need the full trace to explain why a decision went the way it did — especially when the decision is "no viable candidate."

## Scope

Add `Candidates []RouteCandidate` to `RouteDecision` where `RouteCandidate` is a public mirror of `internal/routing.Candidate` exposing:

- `Harness string`
- `Provider string`
- `Endpoint string`
- `Model string`
- `Score float64`
- `Eligible bool`
- `Reason string` (rejection reason when Eligible=false, scoring reason when Eligible=true)

`ResolveRoute` populates `Candidates` in **both** success and error paths. Today's error-returns-nil-decision pattern is changed to return `(*RouteDecision{Candidates: ...}, err)` so callers have the trace even when nothing was pickable.

Wrap the "no viable candidate" typed error (retained per agent-dfabb10b's scope) with a `DecisionWithCandidates` interface (or just a field) so callers can recover the trace via `errors.As`.

## Consumers

- DDx `ddx agent route-status` will render the trace per profile.
- DDx providers UI (ddx-23978824) will render per-harness per-provider rejection heatmap.
- Bead `kind:routing` evidence events will include the full trace, not just the winning pick.
title: Public decision trace: expose RouteDecision.Candidates with per-candidate rejection reasons

acceptance:
1. `go build ./...` succeeds; `go test ./...` passes (no behavior change).
2. `make build` produces binary named exactly `ddx-agent` (same as today).
3. `bash tests/install_sh_acceptance.sh` passes.
4. `internal/productinfo/productinfo.go` exists and exports `Name`, `BinaryName`, `ConfigDir` as `var` (not `const`).
5. Every non-test Go file under `cmd/` and `internal/` that previously contained `"ddx-agent"`, `"ddx-agent-bench"`, or `"ddx-agent-benchscore"` as a literal now references `productinfo.BinaryName` / derived constant / `productinfo.ConfigDir` / `productinfo.Name`. Verify with: `git grep -n '"ddx-agent' -- '*.go' ':(exclude)*_test.go' ':(exclude)internal/productinfo/*'` returns zero hits.
6. `Makefile` contains exactly one literal `ddx-agent` (the `BINARY_NAME := ddx-agent` assignment). Verify: `grep -c 'ddx-agent' Makefile` equals `1`.
7. `install.sh` references `${BINARY_NAME}` for the binary name. Literal `ddx-agent` appears only in the `BINARY_NAME=` assignment and in human prose (comments, success banners). Verify by inspection.
8. `.github/workflows/release.yml` declares `BINARY_NAME` once (at `env:`) and references it via `${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}` or `$BINARY_NAME` in shell steps. Literal `ddx-agent-` appears only in the env declaration or as a hyphenated derivation of the env var.
9. Running `./ddx-agent --version` still shows the version string; `./ddx-agent --help` still shows usage with `ddx-agent` as the program name.
10. State paths unchanged: `~/.config/ddx-agent/`, `~/.local/state/ddx-agent/` still resolve correctly (no existing users lose access to cached state).
description:
Follow-on to `agent-87b1b347` (closed in 794dd1d) — that bead decoupled `cmd/` directory names from the product brand. This bead pushes the decoupling down into Go source and shell/CI literals so product-identity strings live behind a small, replaceable surface.

## Goal

Reduce a future rename to changing 3 constants + module path + prose, with zero risk of missed call sites hidden in error messages, prompts, or state paths.

## Design: one Go package, one shell variable

**New Go package: `internal/productinfo/productinfo.go`**

```go
package productinfo

// Values stay at their current literals for this bead — no behavior change.
// A future rename changes these here; call sites update automatically.
var (
    Name       = "DDx Agent"   // human-readable prose
    BinaryName = "ddx-agent"   // executable name, log prefixes, state dirs
    ConfigDir  = "ddx-agent"   // subdir under ~/.config/, ~/.local/state/, XDG paths
)
```

Make them `var` (not `const`) so future versions can ldflags-inject or test-override. No ldflags injection in this bead — just the indirection.

**Shell/CI: one variable at the top of each file**

- `Makefile`: `BINARY_NAME := ddx-agent` near VERSION/BUILD_TIME; replace literals with `$(BINARY_NAME)`
- `install.sh`: `BINARY_NAME="${BINARY_NAME:-ddx-agent}"` near top; replace literals with `${BINARY_NAME}` (keep human prose like `"# DDX Agent installer"` as-is)
- `.github/workflows/release.yml`: add `env: BINARY_NAME: ddx-agent` at job level; replace literals with `${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}` in shell steps

## In-scope Go call sites (20 refs across 10 files)

All of these hardcode `"ddx-agent"` or `"ddx-agent-bench"` / `"ddx-agent-benchscore"` literals. Migrate to `productinfo.BinaryName` (possibly with a `BenchBinaryName` derived constant `BinaryName + "-bench"` etc., or three separate constants — implementer's call):

**CLI flag sets and exec calls:**
- `cmd/agent/main.go:49` — `flag.NewFlagSet("ddx-agent", ...)`
- `cmd/agent/update.go:190` — `exec.Command("which", "ddx-agent")`

**Bench/benchscore usage strings:**
- `cmd/bench/main.go:1,32,39,46` — package comment + usage/error strings
- `cmd/bench/discovery.go:101` — error printf
- `cmd/bench/report.go:35,46,52` — error printf
- `cmd/bench/runner.go:191,198,202,209,225,233,237,245,252,297` — error printf + `Name: "ddx-agent-bench"` struct field
- `cmd/benchscore/main.go:17,23,29` — usage/error strings

**State/config directory paths (most user-visible):**
- `internal/harnesses/claude/quota_cache.go:52,58` — `filepath.Join(xdg, "ddx-agent", ...)` and `filepath.Join(home, ".local", "state", "ddx-agent", ...)`
- `internal/harnesses/codex/quota_cache.go:32,38` — same pattern
- `internal/observations/observations.go:244,248,250` — `~/.config/ddx-agent/observations.yaml`

Use `productinfo.ConfigDir` here (not `BinaryName`) so the two can diverge if needed.

**Harness registry self-entry:**
- `internal/harnesses/registry.go:97` — `Binary: "ddx-agent"`

**Prompt presets (agent identifies itself to LLM):**
- `internal/prompt/presets.go:76` — `"Balanced, tool-aware prompt — the DDX Agent default"`
- `internal/prompt/presets.go:115` — `"DDX Agent benchmark mode — non-interactive, optimized for evaluation"`
- `internal/prompt/presets.go:116` — prompt body `"You are a coding agent running inside DDX Agent in benchmark mode..."`

Use `productinfo.Name` for prose-style references.

**Doc comments (low priority, update opportunistically):**
- `service.go:16,94,352` — `// DdxAgent is the entire public Go surface...`, `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/ddx-agent/config.yaml`, `CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md`
- `cmd/catalogdist/main.go:24` — flag description `"Minimum compatible ddx-agent version"`

## Shell/CI call sites

**`Makefile` (2 refs):**
- line 9: `go build $(LDFLAGS) -o ddx-agent ./cmd/agent`
- line 98: `rm -f ddx-agent`

**`install.sh` (~17 refs):** lines 2, 22, 26, 30, 34, 66, 103, 110, 112, 116, 118, 154, 165, 167, 189, 190, 194, 195, 205, 208, 209. Most expand `BINARY_NAME`; the "DDX Agent installer" comment and "🎉 DDX Agent installed successfully" banner stay as human prose.

**`.github/workflows/release.yml` (7 refs):** lines 48, 53, 54, 73, 74, 78, 82. All expand `${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}` or shell `$BINARY_NAME`.

## Explicitly out of scope

- **Module path `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent`** — separate concern; if we adopt a stable codename for the module path (see product decisions), that's a different bead.
- **README, CONTRIBUTING, CHANGELOG, website prose** — inherently human-facing; the rename PR handles these.
- **HELIX docs under `docs/helix/**`** — rename sweep handles, not in refactor scope.
- **Test fixtures using `"DDX Agent"` as example config values** (`internal/config/config_test.go:304`, `cmd/agent/cli_process_test.go:351`, `occompat/occompat_test.go:158`) — these verify parser behavior on arbitrary user-provided strings; the "DDX Agent" value is incidental, not a product-name reference.
- **Test assertions that check specific binary-name output** — update only if they fail after source migration; otherwise leave as behavioral assertions.
- **Config/cache path migration shims** — not needed in this bead (values unchanged). Will be needed when the rename bead changes `ConfigDir` value.
- **ldflags-based injection of productinfo vars** — keep it simple; just the indirection here.

## Why not `const`?

Using `var` leaves the door open for future ldflags-injection (`-X productinfo.BinaryName=foo`) without a schema change. No actual injection added here.

## Rollback

All changes are additive (new package) + mechanical string-to-symbol replacements. Revert the commit if anything breaks. No migrations, no external state, no user-visible behavior change.
title: Consolidate product-identity strings behind productinfo package and BINARY_NAME var

acceptance: go test ./... passes; new test TestRun_MidTurnOverflowRetry exists in loop_test.go and covers: (1) provider returns context-overflow error on first call mid-turn, (2) compaction runs, (3) provider is called again with compacted history, (4) turn completes successfully with no ErrContextOverflow returned to caller
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>go-std | testing</concerns>
<practices>gofmt · go vet · golangci-lint · gosec · govulncheck · go test · go test -race · testify/assert for assertions</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 — OTel GenAI spans and JSONL session log are separate observability surfaces; error paths must record spans with error semantics</adrs>
<governing>SD-006 §Mid-iteration check — after tool results are appended, check context usage; if overflow, compact in-place and retry the same turn without aborting. FEAT-001 AC-FEAT-001-03 — transient retry and error paths must not issue extra provider calls beyond policy.</governing>
</context-digest>

Currently loop.go handles context overflow by compacting and then aborting the turn. Codex discovered that aborting is unnecessary — after compaction succeeds, the same provider call can be retried within the same turn.

What to change in loop.go:
- In the mid-turn overflow recovery path (after IsContextOverflowError is detected and compaction runs), instead of returning an error, retry the pending provider call with the compacted history.
- Cap mid-turn retries at 1 (if the compacted history still overflows, abort with error to avoid infinite retry).
- Emit EventCompactionStart / EventCompactionEnd as today; add a mid_turn: true field on the compaction events so callers can distinguish mid-turn from pre-turn compaction.
- All existing overflow/compaction tests must still pass.
