description:
Expose reasoning control through the standalone CLI and use catalog defaults when the caller does not specify reasoning. Current state: `cmd/ddx-agent/main.go` parses `--provider`, `--model`, `--model-ref`, etc., but no `--reasoning`; it resolves catalog model refs and builds `agent.Request` without reasoning. The catalog dependency should expose `SurfacePolicy.ReasoningDefault`.

Required behavior:
- Add one public flag: `ddx-agent run --reasoning <value>`. Named values: `auto|off|low|medium|high`; accept aliases `none` and `false` as `off`; accept provider/harness-supported extended values such as `xhigh`/`x-high` and `max`; accept numeric values such as `0`, `2048`, `8192`.
- Numeric 0 means explicit off. Positive integers mean explicit max reasoning tokens/provider-equivalent numeric value.
- Do not add `--reasoning-budget`; numeric values are passed through `--reasoning`.
- Empty flag means unspecified. `auto` means defer to catalog/provider defaults.
- If a model ref resolved through the catalog has `SurfacePolicy.ReasoningDefault`, use it when CLI reasoning is unspecified/auto.
- If the caller specifies `--reasoning`, that value overrides catalog and provider defaults, including values above high or explicit numeric values when supported.
- Pass the resolved reasoning value into `agent.Request` so the embedding/provider plumbing from the dependency bead enforces it.
- Include resolved reasoning in JSON/session metadata if existing output already reports model selection metadata.
- Do not add a public `--thinking` flag.
- Do not add `--effort` as a public CLI alias in this bead. If another bead later proves a consumer migration requirement, it can add a deprecated alias that normalizes into reasoning.

In-scope files:
- cmd/ddx-agent/main.go
- cmd/ddx-agent/*_test.go
- internal/config/config.go if provider-selection return structs need to carry catalog reasoning metadata
- agent.go only if required by dependency API shape

Out-of-scope:
- Subprocess harness service execution.
- DDx execution-loop retry policy.
- Changing preset names or provider import behavior.
title: cli: add --reasoning and resolve model/catalog reasoning defaults for native runs

acceptance:
1. prompt/presets.go exposes preset IDs: cheap, default, smart, minimal, benchmark. Old IDs (agent, claude, codex, cursor) are retained as deprecated aliases that resolve to their new equivalents AND emit a stderr warning 'preset name X is deprecated; use Y instead; this alias will be removed in vZ'.

2. cmd/ddx-agent main.go --preset flag help string lists the new names first; old names documented as deprecated.

3. README.md and docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-003-system-prompts.md updated to use the new names in every example.

4. Unit test asserts that --preset codex still resolves (back-compat) AND emits the deprecation warning.

5. Unit test asserts new names (smart, default, cheap) resolve to the correct preset bodies.

6. CHANGELOG.md entry documenting the rename and the deprecation timeline.

7. Follow-up bead filed for the hard removal of deprecated aliases in a future release.
description:
## Problem

Current preset names (prompt/presets.go, exposed via cmd/ddx-agent main.go --preset flag) are:
  agent | benchmark | claude | codex | cursor | minimal

Five of six are named after OTHER harnesses or products (claude, codex, cursor) or internal labels (agent, minimal). This creates real cognitive-load bugs in practice:

- 'preset codex' on the embedded 'agent' harness is semantically contradictory. 'codex' is a HARNESS (OpenAI's Codex CLI). Using it as a preset name on the ddx-agent invocation implies 'use the embedded agent but pretend to be codex' — why not just use the codex harness directly? The preset name should name an INTENT, not another implementation.

- Downstream config files like DocumentDrivenDX/ddx .ddx/config.yaml end up with lines like:

      harnesses:
        agent:
          binary: ./ddx-agent
          base_args: ['--json', '--preset', 'codex']

  A reader reasonably assumes this routes through codex. It doesn't. It runs the embedded ddx-agent with a prompt template styled in a 'codex-ish' way. Confusing and load-bearing wrong.

- Preset names encode prompt STYLE (instruction verbosity, tool description format, etc.), not provider choice. Calling that style 'codex' pins the meaning to whatever codex happens to do today; when codex changes, the preset either drifts or becomes misleadingly named.

## Proposed rename

Align preset vocabulary with DDx routing profile vocabulary (cheap | fast | smart) and add a 'default' for the middle. Explicit intent, no cross-reference to other tools.

  agent      -> default    (neutral prompt, balanced tool descriptions)
  benchmark  -> benchmark  (unchanged — evals use a named fixture, not a production preset)
  claude     -> smart      (the richest/most-verbose preset today; used when quality matters and tokens are cheap)
  codex      -> cheap      (the terse preset today; used when tokens/latency matter)
  cursor     -> (drop or merge into default) — 'cursor' is a specific IDE product, not a prompt-intent category
  minimal    -> minimal    (unchanged — legitimate style: smallest possible prompt, for latency-critical or low-context use)

Final name set: cheap | default | smart | minimal | benchmark (5, down from 6).

## Migration

1. Add the new names as first-class preset IDs; keep the old names as deprecated aliases that emit a stderr warning on resolution ('preset name 'codex' is deprecated; use 'cheap' — this warning will be fatal in vX.X.X'). Don't break downstream configs at first.

2. Update README.md, SD-003, and any other doc that references the old names.

3. After one release with deprecation warnings, remove the old names and upgrade the warning to an error.

4. DocumentDrivenDX/ddx will update its .ddx/config.yaml in a coordinated commit once new names ship.

## Other issues with preset names

- 'claude' as a preset name pins Claude-specific prompt conventions into a label that will be wrong if Claude's prompting conventions change. 'smart' is future-proof.
- Preset naming is exposed in session logs, bead execution evidence, and routing events. Confusing names poison those artifacts for anyone reading them later.

## Out of scope

- Changing what each preset actually DOES (tool description text, instruction style). Only the names.
- Adding new presets. The rename is a pure renaming exercise until new intent categories emerge.
notes: Follow-up completed in 5dc6ab7: README.md and SD-003 now show the deprecated aliases as compatibility-only, --preset help wording was corrected, unit tests assert the codex alias warning on stderr, follow-up removal bead agent-ff9c0289 was filed, and go test ./... passed.
title: preset: rename harness-flavored preset names to intent-flavored (smart / default / cheap)

acceptance: go test ./tools/... passes; bash tool output >2000 lines is truncated to last 2000 lines with truncation marker; read tool output >50KB is truncated to head with marker; go vet ./... clean
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for simplicity · No speculative abstractions</principles>
<concerns>go-std | testing</concerns>
<practices>Go stdlib · testify/assert</practices>
<governing>SD-001 §agent loop — tool result handling; SD-004 §streaming</governing>
</context-digest>

Cap tool output size before sending to the LLM to prevent context explosion on local models with small context windows.

**Problem**: ddx-agent currently sends raw untruncated tool output to the LLM. A bash command dumping 500KB floods the context window, causing overflow errors or degraded performance on local models.

**Reference implementation**: pi uses `truncateHead()` for file reads (keep first N lines) and `truncateTail()` for bash output (keep last N lines, preserving errors). Emits `[Truncated: X lines omitted]` marker.

**Files to change**:
- `tools/bash.go` (or equivalent tool executor) — truncate stdout/stderr
- `tools/read.go` — truncate file content  
- `tools/grep.go` — truncate grep output
- Add shared `truncate.go` utility in `tools/` package

**Limits** (match pi):
- Max 2000 lines OR 50KB per tool output, whichever hits first
- bash/grep: keep tail (errors are at end)
- read: keep head (file start is most relevant)
- Append `\n[Truncated: N lines omitted]` when truncated
title: Tool output truncation — cap outputs before LLM send

acceptance:
- [ ] CONTRACT-001-ddx-agent-service.md is the sole external-surface authority on the agent side
- [ ] DdxAgent Service interface exposes 7 methods (Execute, TailSessionLog, ListHarnesses, ListProviders, ListModels, HealthCheck, ResolveRoute, RouteStatus) with the input/output types specified in the plan
- [ ] All five subprocess harnesses (claude, codex, opencode, pi, gemini) are ported into agent internal/harnesses/ with behavioral parity tests
- [ ] Native agent harness wins on at least one batch noninteractive task in the integration suite
- [ ] All entanglement docs deleted (CONTRACT-002 in agent; SD-015, SD-015-resolution-path-trace, SD-023, plan-2026-04-08 in DDx)
- [ ] Internal/ tree blocks external imports of compaction, prompt, tool, session, observations, modelcatalog, provider/* (compile-time enforcement)
- [ ] DDx imports nothing from agent except the root agentlib package
- [ ] Integration suite runs across all available harnesses with cost cap and determinism controls
- [ ] All six "badly implemented" routing smells fixed structurally, not patched
- [ ] agent v0.4.0-pre tagged (legacy + new contract); v0.5.0 tagged (legacy gone, internal/ enforced)
description:
Consolidate LLM routing + harness orchestration entirely into ddx-agent. The module becomes the one stop shop for optimally routed one-shot noninteractive agentic prompts. Two roles: (1) direct first-class agent over native providers (bragi, vidar, openrouter, anthropic), designed to win at batch noninteractive; (2) wrapper around subprocess agents (claude, codex, opencode, pi, gemini) for convenience and cost management.

DDx becomes a thin Service consumer with no agent-internal imports. The boundary is one Go interface (DdxAgent, 7 methods) defined in CONTRACT-001. Internal packages move under internal/ — compiler-enforced.

Replaces the spec entanglement that produced the six well-documented routing smells (parallel routing engines, --provider bypass, fragmented fuzzy match, profile-vs-failover split, capability gating asymmetry, health/quota fragmentation). Plan: /tmp/ddx-agent-service-interface-plan.md (in DDx repo for reference).

Cross-tracker: paired with DDx epic for the consumer-side migration. Beads in this epic precede each corresponding DDx bead.
title: Epic: ddx-agent owns LLM execution + routing — single Service contract, harnesses ported, internal/-enforced boundary

description: Replace backend-pool resolution in the runtime-facing config/CLI path with model-route resolution keyed by requested model or resolved canonical target. Implement deterministic candidate selection for priority-round-robin and ordered-failover. Add passive health state so recently failed candidates can be skipped for a cooldown window, but do not block on active probes before every request. Failover must advance only on provider-side availability failures such as transport errors, auth failures, upstream 429/5xx, or unreachable endpoints; prompt-shape, tool-schema, and other deterministic request errors must not fan out across candidates. Persist only the minimal route state needed for repeatable rotation and cooldowns. Background: the user wants to say ddx-agent run --model qwen3.5-27b and have the runtime choose among bragi/vidar/grendel/openrouter based on actual availability, while DDx remains unaware of inner provider candidates.
title: Implement model-route resolver, passive health state, and failover classification

acceptance:
1. When a streaming response contains NotImplementedError: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI (or any NotImplementedError from the provider), the agent emits a structured error with a stable code (e.g. PROVIDER_CAPABILITY_MISSING) and a human-readable message naming the missing capability.
2. The error is non-retryable in the routing layer — agent does not silently retry on the same provider+model.
3. A unit test fixture replays a recorded SSE stream containing this error and asserts the structured classification.
4. Behavior verified against ddx replay of the failing session in sessions.jsonl.
description:
## Symptom

Sessions in `Projects/ddx/.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` aborted mid-stream with:

```
agent: provider error: received error while streaming: {"message":"Error in iterating prediction stream: NotImplementedError: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI"}
```

Observed against `surface=embedded-openai`. The agent surfaces this as a generic "provider error" and aborts the session.

## Why it matters

This is a **server-capability** error — the local OpenAI-compat server (probably mlx_lm / lmstudio variant) cannot handle a quantized rotating KV cache for the model+config combination. The user can fix it (turn off KV quant; use a different cache type; pick a different model). Today the error is opaque, so the user just sees a stream abort and may retry endlessly.

## Proposed fix

In `provider/openai` (or wherever the streaming SSE error is decoded):

1. Detect the `NotImplementedError: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI` substring (or any `NotImplementedError` from the provider) and classify it as a structured error, not a transport error.
2. Emit a clear human message: "Local server does not support quantized rotating KV cache for this model. Disable KV quantization on the server, or use a model/config it supports."
3. Mark the error non-retryable in the routing layer — endless retries waste compute and the failure is deterministic.
4. Optional: add a routing hint so this provider+model combo is skipped on subsequent dispatches in the same session.

## Related

Counterpart bead being filed in lucebox-hub/dflash to add this as a known failure mode the bench should be able to provoke deliberately (it currently passes `enable_thinking: false` to avoid the related "reasoning stall" path, but doesn't surface KV-cache-incompatibility paths).
title: BUG: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI provider error aborts agent without classification

description:
Codex exec --json can emit structured token_count events as event_msg payloads. Current parser in internal/harnesses/codex/stream.go consumes turn.completed.usage and ddx.usage_source, but ignores event_msg token_count. Add support for token_count usage evidence without regressing the existing canonical usage path.

Required behavior:
- Parse JSONL records where type=event_msg and payload.type=token_count.
- Convert payload.info.last_token_usage into normalized usage evidence.
- Preserve existing turn.completed.usage behavior as canonical when both are present. Avoid silent same-source replacement: either make token_count a distinct source or explicitly prefer turn.completed with a disagreement warning when token counts differ.
- If token_count.rate_limits is present during a DDx-owned Codex run, expose enough structured data from the aggregate/final path for a later integration bead to update quota cache; do not write quota cache in this bead unless it is already localized and tested.
- Do not retain raw JSON lines, prompt text, response text, or tool command content as part of usage evidence.

In-scope files:
- internal/harnesses/codex/stream.go
- internal/harnesses/codex/runner.go only if aggregate/final data needs to carry parsed rate-limit evidence
- internal/harnesses/codex/runner_test.go

Out-of-scope:

- Updating durable Codex quota cache from session files.
- Changing service routing or refresh policy.
title: harness-codex: parse token_count usage from exec streams

description:
Issue-2 hook from the upstream ddx-reviewer-context-overflow.md report (codex narrowed scope; the JSON verdict + extractor work is filed upstream as ddx-021bd69b).

Two small contract-level changes that unblock upstream review-pipeline fixes (FEAT-022 / ddx-021bd69b in the ddx repo):

1. ServiceFinalUsage zero vs unknown:
   Today success-final events drop Usage when counts are positive only (service_execute.go:426 and 780 — codex flagged this). A structural test that 'every success-final has Usage' will fail unless zero is distinguishable from unknown. Refactor ServiceFinalUsage so each token count is *int (or has a separate Known bool); document that nil/false = harness did not emit, zero = harness emitted explicit zero. Update emitters across all registered harnesses to preserve provenance: if the underlying provider sent a count, emit the count (including zero); only emit nil/unknown when the harness genuinely cannot tell.

2. Empty final_text doc convention:
   Add CONTRACT-003 prose stating that 'success + empty final_text' is a valid outcome for legitimate cases (model declined to answer, refusal, structured-output-only response). Consumers MUST NOT treat empty final_text as a transient failure or trigger a retry.

acceptance: models.yaml at schema v4 with per-model entries for all current active targets; manifest.go parses models section; catalog.go exposes AllModelsInTier and LookupModel; PricingFor iterates model entries; UpdateManifestPricing updates per-model costs; v3 manifests load without error via compat upgrade path; go test ./modelcatalog/... covers all new API surfaces.
description:
The current manifest (v3) treats each policy tier (code-high/medium/economy) as the model itself: one cost, one benchmark score, one model per surface. This blocks multi-model tier routing.

Proposed manifest v4 changes:

1. Add a top-level `models` section. Each entry is a concrete model (e.g., qwen3.5-27b, qwen3.5-7b, claude-haiku-5.5) with:
   - family, display_name, tier (reference to parent target), status
   - cost_input_per_m, cost_output_per_m, cost_cache_read_per_m, cost_cache_write_per_m
   - context_window, swe_bench_verified (nullable), live_code_bench (nullable), benchmark_as_of
   - openrouter_id (exact OR model ID)
   - surfaces: map of surface → concrete model ID string

2. Extend target entries with a `candidates` list referencing model IDs from the models section. The ordering is the default capability rank within the tier.

Service.Execute currently dispatches native agent, claude, and codex. The registry and internal harness packages also include pi, opencode, and gemini runners, but the service switch returns a final failure for those harnesses. acceptance:
1. Service native agent execution receives `Reasoning=off|minimal|low|medium|high|xhigh|max|<tokens>` through to `agent.Request` when supported.
2. No separate public `ReasoningBudget` field is added to ServiceExecuteRequest.
3. Legacy `Effort` remains as a deprecated alias for this migration bead; tests prove `Reasoning` wins over `Effort`.
4. Routing test proves `reasoning=off` and numeric 0 do not reject harnesses that lack SupportedEfforts/SupportedReasoning.
5. Routing tests prove extended explicit values such as `minimal`, `xhigh`, and `max` are accepted only for candidates that advertise support and rejected clearly otherwise.
6. Routing tests prove numeric values are accepted for numeric-capable candidates and rejected clearly when over the model/provider max.
7. Harness registry/capability tests prove Codex/Claude/opencode current supported reasoning levels include supported non-standard values such as minimal/xhigh/max instead of being truncated to low/medium/high.
8. Subprocess runner test proves `off`/numeric 0 does not append `--effort`, `--thinking`, `--variant`, or Codex `reasoning.effort` flags.
9. `reasoning=high` still rejects candidates that do not support high reasoning.
10. CONTRACT-003 documents reasoning values, numeric values, alias normalization, adapter-specific mapping, extended levels, and the deprecation of `Effort`.
11. `go test ./internal/routing ./internal/harnesses/... ./...` passes.
description:
Make service execution honor the same reasoning semantics as the CLI/embedding path. Current state: `ServiceExecuteRequest` has `Effort string`, routing gates candidates by supported effort, and subprocess harness requests receive `req.Effort`. The native `agent` harness path in `service_execute.go` builds `agent.Request` without effort/reasoning, so service requests cannot affect native provider thinking. Existing harness terminology can remain adapter-level, but the public contract should expose one reasoning value that supports named and numeric forms.

Current-state note: internal/harnesses currently advertises low/medium/high for Codex and Claude, while modern Codex/Claude-style surfaces may expose values above high (for example x-high/xhigh or max) and opencode already advertises max. This bead must make supported reasoning metadata extensible and current rather than hard-coding low/medium/high.

Required behavior:
- Add `Reasoning` to `ServiceExecuteRequest` as the public field, using the same `Reasoning` typed scalar as the embedding API if possible.
- Do not add a separate public `ReasoningBudget` field. Numeric values are represented by `Reasoning` itself, e.g. `ReasoningTokens(8192)` or string/JSON/YAML scalar `8192`.
- Retain `Effort` as a deprecated alias for one migration window because it already exists in the service contract and harness request path; if both are set, `Reasoning` wins.
- Normalize service reasoning values using the same function as CLI: empty, auto, off, low, medium, high, plus supported extended values such as xhigh/x-high and max; aliases none/false/0 -> off; positive integer -> numeric budget.
- Native service execution passes normalized reasoning into `agent.Request`.
- Routing treats `reasoning=off` and numeric 0 as no extra harness capability requirement. A harness that supports no reasoning/effort flag can still run when reasoning is off.
- Routing must use each candidate/harness/provider advertised supported reasoning levels and numeric budget limits. `low|medium|high`, extended values such as `xhigh`/`max`, and numeric values should gate against support instead of a hard-coded low/medium/high list.
- Refresh harness capability metadata for current Codex/Claude/opencode/Pi/Gemini surfaces where supported, including values above high when the harness accepts them.
- Subprocess harness execution should omit adapter flags for off, unless a harness has a native off flag that is explicitly supported.
- Contract docs describe empty vs auto vs off and explain mapping to harness knobs named effort/thinking/variant or numeric budgets.

In-scope files:
- service.go
- service_execute.go
- service_routing.go
- internal/routing/gating.go
- internal/harnesses/*/runner.go and registry metadata for supported extended values / budget limits
- docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md
- service_*_test.go and internal/routing tests

Out-of-scope:
- CLI flag parsing.
- Model catalog default edits.
- Provider serialization internals beyond using the embedding API.
title: service: propagate ExecuteRequest reasoning into native agent runs and subprocess adapters

description:
Add a privacy-preserving reader for Codex's own session JSONL token_count records. Local inspection showed CODEX_HOME/sessions/**/*.jsonl records such as type=event_msg with payload.type=token_count, payload.info.last_token_usage/total_token_usage, and payload.rate_limits. This reader is for quota/status refresh only, not historical usage reporting.

Required behavior:
- Resolve session root from CODEX_HOME, with a test-only override env var for fixtures.
- Walk sessions bounded: newest files first, skip symlinks, cap file count, bytes per file, and scanner line size. Stop as soon as the newest fresh valid token_count.rate_limits record is found.
- Parse only records where top-level type=event_msg and payload.type=token_count. Ignore every other line without decoding or retaining prompt/response/tool content.
- Use the token_count event timestamp as CapturedAt. If absent or malformed, fallback to file mtime; never use time.Now as the capture time for session-derived evidence.
- Freshness must align with Codex quota cache freshness/debounce policy, currently 15 minutes.
- Map rate_limits primary/secondary by their own window_minutes. Do not hardcode primary=5h or secondary=7d. Use duration-derived display labels only as presentation.
- Preserve used_percent, window_minutes, resets_at, limit_id, and limit_name. Add a dedicated optional LimitName field to harnesses.QuotaWindow if needed.
- Include plan_type from rate_limits as normalized Codex account plan metadata when present. Non-subsidized plans must remain ineligible for automatic routing in existing quota decision code.
- Warnings/errors must be generic and count-based; do not include raw lines or full home paths.

In-scope files:
- internal/harnesses/types.go if LimitName is added
- internal/harnesses/codex/quota_cache.go if snapshot/account shape needs minor extension
- new internal/harnesses/codex/session_token_count.go or equivalent
- new internal/harnesses/codex/session_token_count_test.go
- sanitized fixtures under internal/harnesses/codex/testdata/

Out-of-scope:
- Service refresh integration; a separate bead will wire this reader into refresh order.
- General usage analytics from private Codex sessions.
- Live PTY probing changes.
title: harness-codex: add bounded session token_count quota reader

acceptance:

description:
**The actual goal.** User signal: 'we really need to be able to accomplish useful work using omlx qwen on vidar, we should baseline that with pi, and then tweak our compaction, tools, system prompt and reasoning levels until we get there. The model itself is functional, our harness needs a lot of work.'

This bead is the iteration loop, not a one-shot benchmark.

## Methodology

**Backing.** omlx Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit on vidar (the local omlx host). NOT openrouter — the user's stated target is local omlx.

**Pi configuration.** Pi --list-models on the test machine doesn't currently include omlx. First step: ensure pi is configured to talk to omlx vidar (the user's target). This may require:
- Pi config file edit on the test machine (out of scope for direct manipulation per user directive 'just consume what pi tells us')
- OR a different test machine where pi already has omlx
- OR a pi command-line --provider flag that points at vidar

Capture the pi --list-models snapshot under the omlx-configured pi; that snapshot is the baseline evidence.

**Paired design.** Each task runs on BOTH harnesses with same model, same prompt:
- agent-omlx-vidar-qwen36 (harness=agent, provider=vidar-omlx, model=Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit)
- pi-omlx-vidar-qwen36 (harness=pi, provider=<pi-internal-omlx>, model=Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit)

**Reviewer pin.** Both arms use --review-harness codex --review-model gpt-5.5 (cannot be claude — confound).

**Sample size.** Minimum N=8 paired tasks; target N=12.

**Win condition.** Success rate ≥ pi's. Cost-per-success ≤ 1.2× pi's.

## Iteration loop (the actual work)

The baseline measurement is step zero. After it lands:

1. **Identify top-1 gap** — which lever moved the needle most against agent? Compaction policy, tool exposure, system prompt, or reasoning encoding?
2. **Tweak that lever** — file a small focused bead (e.g., 'compaction: tune Qwen-friendly summary trigger') and implement.
3. **Re-measure** — append a new dated parity row to the tracker (agent-f43d1ed2).
4. **Repeat** until agent matches or beats pi for 3 consecutive measurements.

The iteration beads are filed reactively as data surfaces, not upfront.

## Levers (per user signal)

- **Compaction.** internal/compaction/. May be too aggressive or too lax for Qwen's smaller context strategies.
- **Tools.** internal/tool/. Tool descriptions, parallelism, tool count. Qwen may be confused by tool sets that work fine for Claude/GPT.
- **System prompt.** internal/prompt/presets.go. Default and benchmark presets may be tuned for stronger models.
- **Reasoning levels.** Reasoning encoding through openai-compat to omlx. Qwen3.6 is a thinking-variant model; we may be sending the wrong wire shape.

## Deliverable (initial)

docs/research/AR-2026-04-26-agent-vs-pi-omlx-vidar-qwen36.md with:
1. Methodology section (paired design, N, reviewer pin, win condition).
2. Pi configuration evidence: captured pi --list-models snapshot showing omlx is reachable.
3. Per-task pairwise outcome table: task | agent_status | pi_status | costs | durations | reviewer verdicts.
4. Aggregate metrics + 'winner: agent | pi | tie' line.
5. **Top-3 gaps** identified from the data — which lever moved the needle, ranked.

Subsequent iteration runs append rows to docs/research/agent-vs-pi-parity.md (filed as agent-f43d1ed2).
notes:
Worker on 2026-04-26T11:19Z correctly identified the bead is too broad: AC items 3-6 require multi-hour execution (16 paired runs × 5-30 min each through Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit, plus sandbox setup with multi-GB repo clones). Single execute-bead pass cannot hold an interactive Claude session for that duration.

Splitting into four child beads per worker's recommendation:
- C1 (filed): manifest entries — add agent-omlx-vidar-qwen36 + pi-omlx-vidar-qwen36 arms to scripts/beadbench/manifest-v1.json with reviewer pin (codex/gpt-5.5).
- C2 (filed): doc skeleton + pi config evidence — write methodology section and pi --list-models snapshot to AR doc; deliverable's sections 1-2.
- C3 (filed): run the benchmark — long-running operator-driven, not execute-bead.
- C4 (reactive): iteration tweak beads, filed after C3 produces signal.

Reframing this bead as the iteration-loop epic (parent for the chain). The bead is closed at status level (worker auto-closed via already_satisfied) — leaving closed since the original placeholder served its purpose: surfaced the pi-omlx config blocker, got it resolved, surfaced the wall-clock blocker, and triggered the proper split.
title: harness-parity: native agent vs pi on omlx vidar qwen3.6 — iterate until match

description:
Captures codex Q4 from the reasoning-variants synthesis (2026-04-26). Real bug, low blast radius today.

## Bug

internal/provider/openrouter/openrouter.go:20 declares 'Thinking: true' globally. internal/provider/openai/openai.go:307 emits a reasoning field on the wire for any non-empty Reasoning. For fixed-variant models like qwen/qwen3.6-plus where the wire endpoint can't honor an external reasoning toggle (the model name IS the encoding), this either gets silently ignored by the upstream or returns a confusing error.

Today the bug only triggers if a user explicitly pins:
  Provider=openrouter Model=qwen/qwen3.6-plus Reasoning=non-off
through the native agent. With auto-routing not yet exposing pi-aggregated candidates, this path is rarely taken.

## Fix

Provider/harness adapters consult the model's reasoning_wire field (added by agent-a0160cde) before encoding:
- provider → emit reasoning field as today.
- model_id → strip the reasoning field; model name is the encoding.
- none → strip + reject if user asked for non-off levels (catalog/wire mismatch).

## Blocked on

agent-a0160cde (catalog reasoning_wire field must exist before provider can consult it).

## In-scope files

- internal/provider/openai/openai.go (reasoning field emission at line 307 area)
- internal/provider/openrouter/openrouter.go (capability declaration; may need refinement)
- internal/provider/anthropic/anthropic.go (already has structured reasoning encoding; check it honors model-level wire metadata)
- New tests asserting wire body byte-content for each (control, wire) combination

## Out of scope

- Reasoning=auto resolution before gate (filed separately as agent-tbd-auto)
- Family-grouping in catalog (qwen3.6 with sub-variants for off/high) — deferred per codex
title: provider-wire: enforce reasoning_wire to strip reasoning field on fixed-variant models

description:
DDX Agent is entering build-vs-buy territory for direct PTY harness support. ADR-002 selects direct PTY transport and cassettes; ADR-003 requires a real terminal emulator; ADR-004 now constrains the work so the project does not become a terminal emulator, terminal multiplexer, or IDE terminal. Before implementing internal/pty/session, internal/pty/terminal, or internal/pty/cassette, complete a concrete dependency and project-boundary evaluation.

The evaluation must compare buy/adopt options for: PTY process lifecycle, VT/ANSI terminal rendering, expect/input automation, terminal recording/playback, replay timing, cassette schema compatibility, service-event sidecars, licensing, maintenance health, Go API fit, cross-platform behavior, and testability. Include at minimum creack/pty, Netflix/go-expect, vt10x or a comparable Go terminal emulator, Charmbracelet/x ANSI tooling, asciinema/asciicast, Charmbracelet VHS, xterm.js serialize, and JetBrains JediTerm as a scale/reference example.

Use SPIKE-001 as existing direct-PTY top evidence. If more evidence is needed, extend the spike only enough to answer dependency/API risk; do not implement the production PTY library in this bead.

In-scope files:
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-004-terminal-harness-build-vs-buy.md

Out-of-scope:

description:
Surfaced by DDx consumer review. DDx currently parses provider-native files and endpoints directly to build `ddx doctor`, `ddx agent route-status`, and tier-escalation diagnostics:
- cli/internal/agent/session_evidence.go reads Codex native session JSONL
- cli/internal/agent/routing_signal_adapters.go parses Claude stats/cache files
- cli/internal/agent/routing_signal_http.go hits OpenRouter /auth/key and LM Studio /v1/models directly
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go doctor --routing path reads ~/.config/agent/config.yaml directly

All of that is agent-owned: provider health, quota headroom, token usage windows, endpoint availability, auth status. Consumers shouldn't reimplement per-provider parsers every time a provider changes its file layout or endpoint shape.

## Proposal

Extend ListHarnesses / ListProviders / RouteStatus (or a new dedicated Service method) to expose, per provider and per configured harness:

- quota windows with freshness timestamp and source (e.g. "openrouter /auth/key at 2026-04-20T10:15Z")
- subscription account identity where applicable
- historical usage windows (rolling input/output token counts, cost attribution)
- model endpoint reachability + last successful probe
- last error (type, timestamp, detail)

All normalized — consumers ask for "provider X quota" not "parse Claude's ~/.config/anthropic/account.json".

## Consumer impact

Shrinks DDx ddx-ac5c7fdb (thin-consumer epic), ddx-c0f6b19c and ddx-1cc9933c (Phase 5 deletes depend on routing/state files going away), ddx-bbb65768 (local-first profile diagnostics), ddx-4b59b816 (cost-tiered measurement plumbing).
notes: Consolidated plan order 07. This is the broadest contract bead and should land after capability matrix, PTY/cassette decision, profile projection, and route-attempt feedback. It must not become another consumer-side parser. Surface normalized quota/usage/endpoint/auth/error data through ListHarnesses, ListProviders, RouteStatus, or a dedicated status method. Live tests that require credentials must be opt-in record-mode tests that fail fast when unauthenticated; replay-mode cassettes must run deterministically without credentials.
title: service: expose routing health, quota, and provider-endpoint signals through the Service contract

acceptance:
1. Child beads implement Codex token_count stream parsing, bounded private session quota evidence, refresh integration, and DDx-owned usage status without violating foreground cache-only routing.
2. Each child bead has passing focused tests and `go test ./...` passes after the final child lands.
3. Codex automatic routing uses only fresh, complete, subsidized quota evidence and falls back to PTY refresh when session evidence is absent or stale.
description:
Codex exposes better quota and usage evidence than DDx currently consumes. Current DDx code parses codex exec turn.completed.usage for per-run usage and scrapes interactive /status via PTY for quota. Local Codex sessions under CODEX_HOME/sessions contain structured event_msg payloads where payload.type=token_count; those records include last_token_usage, total_token_usage, rate_limits primary/secondary windows, plan_type, credits, and rate_limit_reached_type.

Reviewed plan with Claude and Codex. Required constraints:
- Do not hardcode primary=5h or secondary=7d; map quota windows from rate_limits.*.window_minutes and only use names like 5h/7d as display labels.
- Do not scan private Codex sessions unbounded. Resolve CODEX_HOME plus a test override env var, sort newest files first, cap file count, bytes, and line size, skip symlinks, and stop after the newest fresh valid token_count.rate_limits.
- Treat Codex session files as privacy-sensitive: parse only type=event_msg with payload.type=token_count, never retain or log prompt/response/tool content, raw lines, or full home paths.
- Use token_count event timestamp as CapturedAt; fallback to file mtime only if needed. Never stamp session-derived evidence with now.
- Align freshness with the existing Codex quota freshness/debounce policy: currently 15 minutes.
- Preserve limit_name by adding a dedicated optional QuotaWindow field rather than overloading Name.
- Keep turn.completed.usage canonical for codex exec usage; token_count.last_token_usage is fallback or distinct evidence with explicit source/disagreement behavior.
- If codex exec --json emits token_count.rate_limits during a DDx-owned run, use that to refresh the Codex quota cache after execution.
- Do not populate HarnessInfo.UsageWindows from private ~/.codex/sessions; historical usage must come from DDx-owned session logs.

This epic tracks the implementation beads that turn those constraints into tested code.

In-scope: internal/harnesses/codex/, internal/harnesses/types.go, service.go/service_providers.go as needed, service/provider tests, docs if implementation changes public status behavior.

Out-of-scope: promoting non-primary harnesses, changing foreground routing to synchronously probe live quota, reading arbitrary Codex conversation content for analytics.
title: epic: improve Codex usage and quota inspection

acceptance:
1. New test go test . -run TestResolveRouteAutoResolvesToTierDefaultBeforeGate covers: Reasoning=auto + profile=cheap → resolved internally to off, gate passes for off-only candidates; Reasoning=auto + profile=smart → resolved to high, gate rejects off-only candidates.
2. Existing routing tests still pass (no regression on the unset-Reasoning path).
3. go test ./... -count=1 passes.
description:
Captures codex Q2 from the reasoning-variants synthesis (2026-04-26).

Today internal/routing/gating.go:23 treats unset/auto/off as 'no requirement.' Per CONTRACT-003:1213, code-high tier defaults to reasoning=high; below-smart tiers default to off. When non-thinking variants land in code-high candidate lists (currently they don't — [opus-4.7, gpt-5.4, gemini-2.5-pro] are all thinking-capable), Reasoning=auto + code-high could pick the off-only variant instead of resolving to high.

## Fix

Resolve Reasoning=auto → tier/profile default to a concrete level BEFORE invoking the capability gate. Order:

1. If Reasoning != auto: use as-is.
2. If Reasoning=auto: resolve to the catalog's tier surface_policy reasoning_default for the requested tier (code-high → high, code-economy → off, etc.).
3. If no default applies: no gate (current behavior).

## Blocked on

agent-a0160cde (catalog reasoning_levels field) — the gate uses reasoning_levels to know what each candidate can satisfy.

## In-scope files

- internal/routing/gating.go (auto-resolution before capability check)
- internal/routing/engine.go (call site of CheckGating)
- service_routing.go (where Reasoning is read from RouteRequest)
- New test asserting auto resolves to tier default before gating.

## Why P4

Hypothetical bug today (no non-thinking variants in code-high). Becomes real when variant-style models land in higher tiers.
title: routing: resolve Reasoning=auto to tier/profile default before capability gate

acceptance:
1. `HarnessEntry.SupportedModels []string` field added. Nil retains "no allow-list" semantics.
2. `buildRoutingInputs` populates `SupportedModels` from `cfg.Models` for every registered harness.
3. `gating.go` gate: `h.SupportedModels != nil && req.Model != "" && req.Model ∉ h.SupportedModels` → ineligible with explicit reason.
4. Gate applies regardless of `ExactPinSupport`. Test asserts `ExactPinSupport: true` + allow-list mismatch = ineligible.
5. Regression test (from description) passes and stays in suite as a named guard (e.g., `TestResolveRoute_GeminiRejectsNonGeminiModel`).
6. Inverse test passes: allow-list match with ExactPinSupport succeeds.
7. No existing `TestResolveRoute_*` or engine tests regress.
8. Live-discovery fuzzy-match path is NOT affected: when a harness has `Providers` with `DiscoveredIDs`, that matching takes precedence over the static allow-list for models served by the provider.
9. Rejection reason surfaces in `Candidate.Reason` so callers can log which gate failed.
description:
## Confirmed by code inspection

`HarnessEntry` in `internal/routing/engine.go` has no field for the harness's supported-models allow-list. `internal/routing/gating.go:88` checks only `ExactPinSupport` when validating a model pin. `engine.go:456` `resolveModel` returns `req.Model` directly when no provider discovery is present. Result: any harness with `ExactPinSupport: true` and `Available: true` is eligible for **any** model, regardless of the allow-list declared in `internal/harnesses/registry.go`.

Concrete failure observed on a DDx consumer (2026-04-22): `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "minimax/minimax-m2.7")` returned `harness: gemini` on a machine where gemini CLI was installed. Gemini's registry declares `Models: [gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite]` — explicitly excluding minimax. The engine ignored the allow-list because it never reached the routing layer. DDx consumer then invoked the gemini CLI with the minimax model; CLI rejected it; escalation retried 19 times; 19 bead claims burned.

## Scope

1. Extend `HarnessEntry` in `internal/routing/engine.go` with a `SupportedModels []string` field (nil = no allow-list / rely on provider-side validation).
2. Populate `SupportedModels` in `service_routing.go`'s `buildRoutingInputs` from each harness's `cfg.Models`.
3. In `internal/routing/gating.go`, add a gate: when `h.SupportedModels != nil` AND `req.Model != ""` AND `req.Model ∉ h.SupportedModels`, mark candidate ineligible with reason `"model not in harness allow-list"`. This gate applies **regardless** of `ExactPinSupport` — `ExactPinSupport: true` means "accepts exact pins within its allow-list," not "accepts anything."
4. For harnesses with `SupportedModels: nil` (e.g., any that genuinely proxy to any provider), behavior unchanged — rely on provider-side validation.
5. Fuzzy matching against provider's `DiscoveredIDs` continues to take precedence when the harness has a provider entry with live discovery.

## Regression test

Seed a routing.Inputs with:
- `gemini` harness, Available=true, ExactPinSupport=true, SupportedModels=[gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite]
- `agent` harness with a live endpoint whose DiscoveredIDs contains `minimax/minimax-m2.7`

`Resolve(Request{Profile: "default", Model: "minimax/minimax-m2.7"}, inputs)` must return `Decision{Harness: "agent", ...}`. Must **never** return gemini. Remove the `agent` entry and assert the engine returns `no viable candidate` typed error, not a gemini pick.

Add an inverse test: `Model: "gemini-2.5-flash"` must successfully pick gemini when it's the only viable candidate.
title: Supported-models allow-list in routing inputs; gating rejects non-allowed pins even when ExactPinSupport is true

acceptance:
1. Service exposes RecordRouteAttempt or equivalent with harness, provider, model, endpoint, status, failure reason, duration, and timestamp.
2. ResolveRoute demotes or skips candidates with active failure records inside the configured TTL, while expired records no longer influence routing.
3. RouteStatus reports active route-attempt failure/cooldown state with freshness timestamps and failure reasons.
4. Tests cover failure recording, successful attempt clearing or non-demotion semantics, TTL expiry, same-process reuse, and route status reporting.

description:
Surfaced by DDx consumer review. DDx keeps a process-local provider-health shadow (`cli/internal/escalation/GlobalProviderHealth`) because the Service contract has no public way for a consumer to tell the service "this resolved candidate just failed; please don't hand it back in the next 30 seconds."

The execute-loop needs to know: after a ResolveRoute winner fails with a transient error (provider timeout, stream crash, 502, rate-limit), the NEXT ResolveRoute call within a cooldown window should demote or skip that candidate. Without an upstream feedback path, DDx shadow-tracks it, then has to either re-resolve from scratch or use its own ad-hoc cooldown.

Agent already owns route scoring, quota, and subscription-burn influence. Adding a feedback channel slots cleanly in.

## Proposal

Expose a deterministic feedback method, e.g.:

- Service.RecordRouteAttempt(ctx, RouteAttempt{Harness, Provider, Model, Endpoint, Status, Error, DurationMs}) error

Or event-driven: ServiceEvent of Kind="route-attempt" emitted by Execute can be mirrored by consumers back to Service.ReportRouteFailure for manually-dispatched calls.

Behavior: future ResolveRoute / RouteStatus in the same process (or same persistent state scope) demotes or skips candidates with active failures. Failure records expire on a TTL. RouteStatus reports them.

## Consumer impact

Simplifies DDx ddx-bbb65768 (tier escalation semantics), ddx-4b59b816 (cooldown + measurement). Removes DDx-only GlobalProviderHealth shadow state.
notes: Consolidated plan order 06. This should be implemented before the broad health/quota surface so RouteStatus has a real route-attempt failure primitive to expose. Keep the first version deterministic and local: RecordRouteAttempt or equivalent records keyed harness/provider/model/endpoint, applies TTL, affects same-process ResolveRoute scoring, and is visible in RouteStatus. Persistence can be added only if CONTRACT-003 chooses a state scope.
title: service: add route-attempt feedback (record failure, influence future ResolveRoute)

acceptance:
1. Capabilities.ChatContains, StreamContains, ReasoningContains either removed or made optional with a deprecation comment; default behavior is structural (non-empty content + finish_reason).
2. internal/provider/conformance/run.go subtests no longer fail on a thinking-mode model that emits coherent output without echoing the literal cue.
3. streaming_max_tokens_honored adjusts its cap floor when SupportsThinking=true so reasoning_content has room.
4. Existing shaped-double fixtures across all providers (omlx/lmstudio/lucebox/vllm/openrouter/openai/anthropic) still pass without modification.
5. Re-running TestConformance_OpenAICompatLive/lucebox against bragi:1236 with Qwen3.6-27B-Q4_K_M produces all subtests PASS for the wire-shape ones (health/discovery/chat/streaming/max-tokens/thinking_reasoning/non-streaming_tool_call/multi-tool_wire_shape). The tool_call_streaming and tool_result_roundtrip subtests may legitimately fail if the upstream lucebox build does not yet emit tool calls — this is upstream, not a test-suite issue.
6. Documentation comment on the new structural checks explains why literal-substring assertions were dropped (thinking-mode models do not echo cues verbatim).
description:
The shared conformance suite (internal/provider/conformance/run.go) asserts literal substrings on visible content:

  caps.ChatContains      = "pong"
  caps.StreamContains    = "stream-pong"
  caps.ReasoningContains = "reasoning"

Prompts are framed as 'conformance: stream-pong' / 'conformance: reply with pong' — designed for non-thinking instruct models that will echo the cue verbatim. Thinking-mode local models (Qwen3.x on omlx / lucebox / vllm with the right weights) don't echo arbitrary tokens — they think, then emit their own phrasing. Result: streaming_chat / streaming_max_tokens / tool_call_streaming all FAIL on lucebox + omlx live conformance even when the wire is working correctly.

Empirical 2026-04-27 against bragi:1236 with Qwen3.6-27B-Q4_K_M and 5min timeout / 1024 max_tokens defaults:

  health_check                ✓
  model_discovery             ✓
  non-streaming_chat          ✓ (1024 tokens enough for thinking + 'pong' to land)
  streaming_chat              ✗ stream content "", want substring "stream-pong"
  streaming_max_tokens_honored ✗ max_tokens stream returned empty content
  thinking_reasoning          (in progress)
  tool_call_streaming         ✗ stream did not emit a tool call id

The 'streaming_max_tokens_honored' test sets max_tokens=3 (cap test) — guaranteed to fail on any thinking-mode model regardless of prompt design, since the cap is hit during reasoning_content before any visible content emits. That subtest needs separate handling for thinking-capable providers.

## What we want from the conformance suite

Wire-shape validation: did stream/non-stream/tool-call/finish-reason/usage chunks come through correctly? NOT: does the model say 'pong'?

The current design conflates the two — the literal substring is a proxy for 'wire delivered something' but only works on echo-friendly models.

## Proposed redesign

1. Drop literal-substring assertions for the streaming and non-streaming chat subtests. Replace with structural checks: 'stream emitted at least one delta with non-empty visible content AND a finish_reason'. That covers the wire and is model-agnostic.

2. For thinking-capable providers: assert reasoning_content chunks arrive separately from content chunks (already partly tested by 'thinking reasoning' subtest — extend coverage). Don't require literal substrings here either.

3. 'streaming max_tokens honored': for thinking-capable providers, raise the cap floor (e.g., 256) so reasoning + a token or two of content fits, AND assert the bound holds. For non-thinking, keep the 3-token cap.

4. 'tool_call_streaming': lucebox's current build appears to not yet emit tool_calls (separate upstream concern; the user said tool support is being added). Once the upstream supports it, this should work. No change here — file the lucebox tool-support gap separately if it persists after the next lucebox build.

## Implementation

internal/provider/conformance/run.go:

- Replace `Capabilities.ChatContains` / `StreamContains` with `ChatRequireContent bool` (default true) — checks 'len(content) > 0', not literal match.
- Add `IsThinkingCapable bool` derived from caps.SupportsThinking; for thinking-capable, raise the streaming-max-tokens cap floor.
- 'thinking reasoning' subtest: assert ReasoningContent chars > 0, drop the literal "reasoning" substring check — same brittleness.
- Existing shaped-double fixtures keep emitting "pong" / "stream-pong"; new structural checks pass against them too because content is non-empty.

## Out of scope

- New subtests for tool calling: covered by the just-added multi-tool / non-streaming / tool-result subtests (commit 72399fa).
- Lucebox tool-call support: upstream concern, file separately if it remains absent after the rebuild.
notes: Landed in commit bdc67c4. Removed forced literal-substring auto-defaults; structural checks (non-empty visible content + finish_reason) replace them on the live path; shaped-double descriptors opt back in by setting ChatContains/StreamContains/ReasoningContains explicitly. streaming_max_tokens cap floored at 256 for SupportsThinking providers since 3-token cap is incompatible with reasoning_content emission.
title: test(conformance): prompts brittle for thinking-mode local models — redesign for thinking-aware checks

acceptance:
1. Child beads exist for spec alignment, embedding/provider plumbing, CLI/service plumbing, catalog defaults, execution-policy documentation, and post-migration alias cleanup.
2. Dependencies force spec alignment before code work and force catalog defaults before CLI/service routing tests that depend on them.
3. Final implementation proves with `go test ./...` that `reasoning=off` disables native thinking/reasoning budgets and tier defaults below smart resolve to off.
4. Final implementation proves explicit numeric reasoning values and supported non-standard values such as minimal/xhigh/max are preserved or rejected clearly based on provider/harness capability.
5. Cleanup bead removes or time-bounds legacy public `effort`/`thinking` naming so the repo does not keep permanent dual public terminology.
description:
Unify user-facing reasoning control around one public scalar `reasoning`, not `effort`, provider-specific `thinking`, or split `reasoning_level`/`reasoning_budget` knobs. Today the repo has partial pieces: `agent.ThinkingLevel` and provider config `thinking_level`/`thinking_budget`; subprocess harnesses expose native dials named effort/thinking/variant; the model catalog has `effort_default`; but request-level off cannot override a provider-level positive thinking budget, CLI execution has no reasoning flag, native agent service execution does not pass reasoning into `agent.Run`, and catalog defaults currently make Qwen economy models high-effort.

Policy decision:
- User-facing API is one value: `reasoning`.
- `reasoning` accepts named values such as `auto`, `off`, `low`, `medium`, `high`, and provider/harness-supported extended values such as `xhigh`/`x-high` and `max`.
- `reasoning` also accepts numeric values such as `0`, `2048`, or `8192`; numeric 0 means explicit off and positive integers mean explicit max reasoning tokens or a documented provider-equivalent numeric value.
- `reasoning` is portable intent. Internally it may map to provider/model knobs named `reasoning`, `thinking`, `effort`, `variant`, a token budget, or may be dropped entirely for models/providers that do not support explicit reasoning control when the value came from auto/default policy.
- Explicit unsupported named values or over-limit numeric values must fail clearly rather than silently downgrade.
- Provider-specific `thinking` remains an implementation detail used only when a provider/flavor supports the wire field.
- Existing service/harness `Effort` fields may remain as compatibility/adaptor terminology during migration, but new public CLI/config/catalog wording should prefer `reasoning`.
- Default for tiers below smart/code-high is `reasoning=off`, including Qwen local/economy models such as Qwen3.6/Qwen3.5.
- Smart/code-high defaults to `reasoning=high`, but explicit caller settings always win, including supported values above high or numeric budgets.
- Bead execution strategy should try cheap or standard models with `reasoning=off` first; only retry with smart if the first pass fails for eligible model/implementation reasons.

This epic owns the agent-side work: public API, CLI/service plumbing, provider serialization, catalog defaults, and tests. The DDx execution-loop fallback policy is tracked in the DDx repo as paired consumer bead `ddx-785d02f7`.

In-scope files across children:
- agent.go, loop.go
- service.go, service_execute.go, service_routing.go
- internal/reasoning/*
- internal/harnesses/* capability metadata and runners
- cmd/ddx-agent/main.go and CLI tests
- internal/provider/openai/openai.go and provider tests
- internal/modelcatalog/* and catalog YAML
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-003-providers.md
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-model-routing.md
- docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-005-provider-config.md

Out-of-scope:
- Moving packages under internal/
- The openai provider refactor into per-flavor packages
- Full live provider conformance; use shaped tests here and let agent-8e0dcca1 cover live matrix expansion
- DDx repo execute-loop implementation, except documenting the paired bead.
title: Epic: unify reasoning policy across CLI, embedding, routing, providers, and bead execution defaults

SD-004-streaming.md defines the StreamDelta struct without the ReasoningContent field. The shipped stream.go has:

    ReasoningContent string `json:"reasoning_content,omitempty"`  // thinking/reasoning tokens for extended-reasoning models

This field is consumed in stream_consume.go to implement ErrReasoningOverflow and ErrReasoningStall detection. SD-004 is therefore stale with respect to the actual StreamDelta type.

Work: Add ReasoningContent to the StreamDelta struct definition in SD-004, with a note on its role in loop-detection behavior. Cross-reference the reasoning-overflow/stall heuristics.

Discovered by alignment review agent-35bf3d08, review issue agent-ecc79bf0.
notes: Already satisfied at 2026-04-17. SD-004-streaming.md line 40-47 defines StreamDelta.ReasoningContent with a docstring that cross-references ErrReasoningOverflow/ErrReasoningStall. Guards are fully documented at lines 135-152 (pure-reasoning-mode window, byte/stall thresholds, sentinel errors). No additional work required.
title: docs: update SD-004 StreamDelta definition to include ReasoningContent field

acceptance:
1. ServiceExecuteRequest has CachePolicy string field; AST-checked by go test . -run TestServiceExecuteRequestHasCachePolicy.
2. RouteRequest has the same field; AST-checked by go test . -run TestRouteRequestHasCachePolicy.
3. Unknown CachePolicy values (anything other than "", "default", "off") return a clear error from svc.Execute before dispatch; covered by go test . -run TestServiceExecuteRequestRejectsUnknownCachePolicy.
4. CONTRACT-003 documents the field with at least one paragraph naming the use cases (deterministic eval, privacy-sensitive, one-shot benchmark) and stating the default-empty behavior.
5. go test ./... -count=1 passes.
description:
Bead A of the prompt-caching plan (codex-refined). Add a public CachePolicy field on ServiceExecuteRequest and RouteRequest plumbed through to provider opts. No provider acts on it yet — beads C (Anthropic cache_control writes) and D (cache-aware cost attribution) consume it.

Concrete edits:
- service.go: add CachePolicy string to ServiceExecuteRequest and RouteRequest. Valid values: "" / "default" / "off". Unknown values rejected at the boundary.
- service_execute.go: thread CachePolicy through to the routing.Request and into provider opts (agent.Options or equivalent — check what the providers consume today).
- internal/provider/anthropic/anthropic.go and internal/sdk/openaicompat/client.go: define how each provider receives the policy (via opts field). Do NOT change behavior in this bead; just add the wiring point.
- docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md: prose paragraph naming the opt-out cases (deterministic eval, privacy-sensitive prompts, one-shot benchmark runs); mention that the field defaults to empty meaning "default caching" — the actual cache behavior is per-provider and is set by beads C and D.

Why: Without an opt-out, callers running deterministic evals or privacy-sensitive prompts cannot disable caching. Filing the contract now means beads C and D land with the opt-out already in place — no contract churn between commits.

In-scope files:
- service.go (struct fields)
- service_execute.go (threading)
- service_routing.go (RouteRequest threading)
- internal/provider/anthropic/anthropic.go (opts wiring point)
- internal/sdk/openaicompat/client.go (opts wiring point)
- docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md (prose)

Out of scope:
- Anthropic cache_control writes (bead C)
- Cost-attribution wiring (bead D)
- OpenAI prefix-stability test (bead B)

title: cache: ServiceExecuteRequest.CachePolicy public opt-out

acceptance: TestResolveExplicitHarnessPiProviderPinAcceptsAnyModel passes. Manual: ddx work --harness pi --provider omlx-vidar-1235 --model Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit dispatches without an ErrHarnessModelIncompatible error from the routing layer.
description:
Pi has a model-validation bypass at service_execute.go:266
modelSupportedForHarness allowing any model when --provider is set
(\"pi CLI owns per-provider model validation\"). But internal/routing/engine.go
duplicates the same model-vs-SupportedModels gate inside pinValidation
and didn't honor the same exception, so the routing engine rejected
pi+qwen with ErrHarnessModelIncompatible before service_execute could
apply its allowance.

Reproducer (pre-fix):

    ddx work --harness pi --provider omlx-vidar-1235 \\
      --model Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit --review-harness claude

Result:

    agent: execute: model \"Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit\" is not supported by
    harness \"pi\"; supported models: gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-pro

Defeats the entire point of pi's provider-pin support added in v0.9.9.

Fix shipped in 605729c (this bead) — added the same canonicalHarness
== \"pi\" && req.Provider != \"\" bypass to internal/routing/engine.go
pinValidation, plus a regression test
TestResolveExplicitHarnessPiProviderPinAcceptsAnyModel that covers
both branches.
title: routing: pi-harness model-validation gate at engine layer doesn't honor provider-pin bypass that exists at service_execute

description: Make hard pin semantics explicit and tested before power scoring is added. In-scope: internal/routing and service routing tests. Exact Model pins constrain model identity; Provider pins constrain provider identity; Harness pins constrain harness identity. The router may optimize availability/cost only inside the pinned axes. If no candidate satisfies the pins, it returns a detailed no-candidate error instead of selecting a different model/provider/harness.
notes:

Improvement captured before implementation: add negative tests where an attractive available unpinned candidate exists, so assertions prove the router does not silently substitute outside pinned axes.

Implementation notes: Provider is now an exact provider pin, with non-matching candidates rejected as pin_mismatch. NoViableCandidateError includes requested model/provider/harness pins. Added regression coverage for provider-only pin, unsupported exact model pin, harness pin, and route-attempt cooldown behavior under automatic routing vs explicit provider pin.

Verification: go test ./internal/routing -run 'Explicit.*Pin|SmellProvider|NoViableCandidate|GeminiRejectsNonGeminiModel' -count=1; go test ./internal/routing -count=1; go test . -run 'ResolveRoute|RouteCandidate|ExplicitHarness|NoViable|Pin' -count=1; go test ./...

acceptance: Unit test feeds a stream of pure reasoning_content deltas past the threshold and asserts the stream read returns the sentinel error. The agent loop treats the error as non-retryable and exits cleanly (exit code 0 in benchmark mode). Both the byte-threshold and stall-timer paths are exercised.
description:
When a thinking model generates reasoning tokens without ever producing output content or tool calls, the agent hangs until the external timeout (Harbor's asyncio.wait_for) fires. Observed in cancel-async-tasks: qwen3.5-27b emitted 221k reasoning chars over 1800s, rewrote the same solution 128 times, and never finished.

The agent loop should detect this condition and abort the stream early rather than waiting for the provider or harness to kill it.

Detection heuristics (implement at least one, preferably both):
1. Reasoning-token threshold: if accumulated reasoning content exceeds a configurable limit (default ~32k chars) with no content or tool_call delta yet seen, cancel the stream and return a sentinel error (ErrReasoningOverflow or similar).
2. Reasoning-stall timer: if only reasoning_content deltas arrive for longer than a configurable duration (default 120s) with no content or tool_call delta, cancel the stream.

The agent loop should treat ErrReasoningOverflow as a non-retryable loop-termination condition — log a warning and stop iterating rather than retrying.

Files likely involved: stream.go or stream_consume.go (stream reading), loop.go (error handling).
notes: Verified shipped in v0.3.1: stream_consume.go (ErrReasoningOverflow/Stall, reasoningByteLimit, reasoningStallTimeout); tested in stream_consume_test.go:483/530/507/597. Closing per AR-2026-04-12 agent-39586e24.
title: fix: detect runaway reasoning loop and abort stream early

acceptance: go test ./... passes; CompactionConfig gains UserMessageTailTokens field (default 20000); new test TestCompact_UserMessageTailReInclusion exists and verifies that after compaction the replacement history contains both the summary message and the most recent real user messages up to the token budget, in chronological order
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>go-std | testing</concerns>
<practices>gofmt · go vet · golangci-lint · gosec · govulncheck · go test · go test -race · testify/assert for assertions</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 — OTel GenAI spans and JSONL session log are separate observability surfaces</adrs>
<governing>SD-006 §Summary Injection — the compaction summary replaces all compacted messages. SD-006 §Codex research — keeps up to 20K tokens of recent user messages alongside summary. FEAT-001 AC-FEAT-001-06 — compaction no-fit paths must fail closed.</governing>
</context-digest>

Currently compaction/compaction.go replaces compacted history with only the summary message. Codex retains the most recent real user messages (up to a token budget) alongside the summary so the model retains actual request history.

What to change in compaction/compaction.go (or the Compactor function):
- After generating the summary, collect real user-role messages from the tail of the pre-compaction history (exclude system prefix messages, previous compaction summaries, and tool result messages — only real user messages).
- Accumulate messages from newest to oldest until a configurable token budget is exhausted (default: 20000 tokens, chars/4 heuristic). Per SD-006 §Overlong individual user messages, truncate individual messages that exceed the budget rather than dropping them.
- The replacement history becomes: [summary_message, ...tail_user_messages] ordered so the summary is first and user messages follow in chronological order.
- Expose the budget as a new field UserMessageTailTokens int in CompactionConfig (default 20000; 0 = disabled).
- All existing compaction tests must still pass.
- Add test TestCompact_UserMessageTailReInclusion verifying tail messages appear in replacement history.

Governing specs: SD-006, FEAT-001.
title: feat: compaction user-message tail re-inclusion after compact

acceptance:
1. Bash output filtering is configurable and defaults to current behavior unless explicitly enabled.
2. RTK mode uses an allowlisted rewrite/proxy path for at least `git status` and `go test` and is covered by tests with a fake rtk binary.
3. Missing/unusable RTK falls back to current raw bash execution with a clear marker and without changing command exit-code semantics.
4. Exit code, stderr, timeout/cancellation behavior, and nonzero-command handling are preserved.
5. Oversized filtered and fallback stdout/stderr remain bounded with clear filtered/truncated markers and raw-output recovery text if a raw-output path is implemented.
6. Documentation states mode, tradeoffs, and that built-in read/find/grep tools are not automatically RTK-filtered.
7. go test ./internal/tool ./cmd/ddx-agent ./... passes.
description:
Implement the bash output filtering slice from docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-19-provider-routing-tool-output.md.

Filtering is opt-in and scoped to the bash tool boundary. Built-in read/find/grep/ls-style tools are not filtered in this slice. RTK mode may use an installed `rtk` binary for allowlisted commands such as `git status` and `go test`; missing or failing RTK falls back to current bash behavior with a marker. Agent does not install RTK, run `rtk init`, collect RTK analytics, or mutate shell configuration.
title: tool: add optional RTK-style bash output filtering

acceptance:
1. `git submodule update --init scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2` succeeds and the canary task directories exist directly under scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2.
2. Required runtime prerequisites are present before the paid run: `test -n "$OPENROUTER_API_KEY"`, `command -v harbor`, `command -v pi`, `command -v opencode`, and `command -v ddx-agent-bench` all pass.
3. A paid Phase A.1 matrix run completes with harnesses ddx-agent, pi, and opencode against profile gpt-5-mini using `--tasks-dir=scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2`.
4. The resulting matrix artifact contains real harness executions and no prerequisite-only/install_fail_permanent matrix caused by missing task directories.

6. Commit benchmark-results/matrix-<timestamp>/matrix.json, matrix.md, costs.json, and a dated memo under docs/research/ that cites the run and states whether the Phase A.1 matrix executed without prerequisite failures.

description:
Follow-up epic for root causes that prevented the Phase A.1 live harness matrix from meeting acceptance in this environment. Preflight on 2026-04-30 found OPENAI_API_KEY unset, harbor missing, and opencode missing; pi was installed. The prerequisite child beads are now closed, but prior review evidence shows the committed matrix artifact benchmark-results/matrix-20260504T044909Z is invalid because it reports 27/27 install_fail_permanent from a stale tasks-dir path.

This epic is now manual benchmark closure work, not normal execute-loop work. The remaining action is to run the paid Phase A.1 matrix in an environment with OPENROUTER_API_KEY, harbor, pi, opencode, ddx-agent-bench, and the built Fizeau artifact available. Use the corrected tasks directory: scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2, not scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2/tasks.

Expected command shape:
HARBOR_AGENT_ARTIFACT="$PWD/fiz-linux-amd64" ddx-agent-bench matrix --subset=scripts/beadbench/external/termbench-subset-canary.json --profiles=gpt-5-mini --harnesses=ddx-agent,pi,opencode --reps=3 --tasks-dir=scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2 --per-run-budget-usd=1.00 --budget-usd=32.40 --out=benchmark-results/matrix-<timestamp>

Then run:
ddx-agent-bench matrix-aggregate benchmark-results/matrix-<timestamp>

Commit benchmark-results/matrix-<timestamp>/matrix.json, matrix.md, costs.json, and an updated docs/research/matrix-baseline-phase-a1-2026-05-04.md or new dated memo citing the valid run.

NON-SCOPE
Do not close this epic based only on prerequisite availability or on install-fail artifacts. Do not rerun with the stale /tasks suffix.
notes: SUPERSEDED 2026-05-07: Operator decision is to migrate benchmark runners to TerminalBench 2.1 and then run a full sweep instead of spending the next paid/manual window on the stale Phase A.1 TB-2.0 matrix. Replacement work: fizeau-ee77ec7f (migrate runners to TerminalBench 2.1) and fizeau-c770193a (run full TerminalBench 2.1 sweep). Do not use the old TB-2.0 tasks-dir acceptance as a current benchmark gate.

acceptance: fiz running inside TB-2 Docker containers can successfully reach OpenRouter API; overfull-hbox no longer fails with x509 error
description:
Some TB-2 Docker images do not include root CA certificates (e.g., ca-certificates package). When fiz runs inside those containers and tries to reach https://openrouter.ai, the TLS handshake fails with 'x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'. Result: 0 tokens consumed, task immediately fails.

Observed on: overfull-hbox (LaTeX debugging task). May affect other tasks with minimal Docker images.

Fix options:
1. harbor_agent.py: install ca-certificates in the container before running fiz (apt-get install -y ca-certificates)
2. fiz: add TLS skip-verify flag for benchmark preset (not recommended)
3. Detect and skip tasks with this error, flag them as infra issues
notes:
## TB-2 Evidence
overfull-hbox (easy/debugging) fails with TLS error: 'tls: failed to verify certificate: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'. 0 tokens consumed. The task Docker image lacks root CA certificates. See: benchmark-results/iteration-v1/sonnet-runs/cells/fiz/claude-sonnet-4-6/rep-001/overfull-hbox/
title: BENCH: Docker containers lack root CA certs; fiz cannot reach OpenRouter

description:
Step 2 of ADR-005 migration. Wire the public auto-selection inputs from step 1 (agent-ddcc903b) into the existing internal/routing engine, replace the per-tier adaptive min-tier window with a per-(provider,model) signal, and redesign 'ddx agent route-status' to show eligible candidates per intent.

Engine wiring (filter gates already exist in internal/routing/engine.go:15 — internal/routing.Request.EstimatedPromptTokens and RequiresTools — they just need to be plumbed):

1. service_routing.go: pass req.EstimatedPromptTokens and req.RequiresTools through ResolveRoute into the routing.Request the engine builds. Verify the engine's existing filter logic actually fires (write tests if absent).
2. service_routing.go: extend the liveness filter — when the candidate set is empty after filtering by liveness AND by EstimatedPromptTokens/RequiresTools, escalate the tier ceiling once (e.g. code-medium → code-high). If escalation also empties, return a precise error: 'no live provider supports prompt of N tokens with tools=B at tier ≥ X'. Replaces the current 'tiers exhausted' jargon.
3. Subscription quota cost ramp at service_routing.go:593 stays unchanged — codex confirmed it's correct as-is.

Per-(provider,model) signal:

4. service_route_attempts.go currently keys success/failure by tier; rekey by (provider, model). Memory + TTL stays the same (persistence deferred). One bad model no longer locks out its whole tier.
5. Delete the per-tier adaptive min-tier window (the 'trailing success rate 0.06 over 17 attempts' lockout we observed). Replace with per-(provider,model) penalty in the existing scoring path.

Route-status redesign:

6. cmd/agent/route_status.go (or wherever 'ddx agent route-status' lives): rewrite to query ResolveRoute with a synthetic request matching the operator's intent (--profile, --model-ref) and display the full ranked candidate list with component scores + filter reasons. Drop any code that enumerates configured model_routes keys.
7. Output format: one line per candidate with provider, model, score, cost, latency, success_rate, filter_reason (if filtered), and an indicator for the dispatch winner. Existing 'ddx agent route' command (if separate) merges into route-status or remains as a JSON-emitting variant.

Beads can run in parallel with step 3 (config + CLI cleanup) once step 1 is done — they touch different files and concerns.

In-scope files:
- service_routing.go (engine wiring, escalation, error message)
- service_route_attempts.go (key by provider+model)
- internal/routing/engine.go and score.go ONLY if the wiring exposes a missing input; do not refactor scoring.
- cmd/agent/route_status.go (or equivalent)
- New service_routing_test.go cases for the gates and escalation.
- New cmd/agent/route_status_test.go for the redesigned output.

Out of scope:
- model_routes config loader deletion (step 3)
- Persistent EWMA / warm-start (deferred per ADR-005)
- OpenRouter / native HTTP live quota (deferred per ADR-005)
notes: Superseded by 4-way split (worker analysis 2026-04-25T09:18Z): agent-c953a473 (ContextWindows wiring), agent-99433b19 (liveness escalation), agent-934fb8a2 (per-(provider,model) signal test), agent-9c9cc191 (route-status redesign, blocked on c953a473). Some scope was already done by step 1 (engine gates exist; per-(provider,model) keying already structurally correct; no per-tier adaptive min-tier remains). Closing as superseded.
title: Wire smart-routing inputs into engine + redesign route-status + per-(provider,model) signal
