description: Introduce per-provider quota state (available -> quota_exhausted -> available) with retry_after timestamp. Routing engine excludes quota_exhausted providers from candidate selection. Define new typed error NoViableProviderForNow returned when Execute has zero eligible providers solely because all are quota_exhausted (i.e., would otherwise be available). Error carries retry_after timestamp (earliest expected provider recovery) and exhausted_providers []string. DDx (downstream) consumes this to pause its drain loop and resume on retry_after. This slice is the foundational plumbing — header parsing, burn-rate tracking, and probe behavior land in sibling beads. Reuses existing provider abstraction; no schema changes needed for state machine itself. Distinct from existing 'no-candidate' / 'config' errors and from the per-request transient feedback path (RecordRouteAttempt).
title: fizeau: provider quota state machine + NoViableProviderForNow error

description:
PROBLEM
  Operators tailing a long fizeau-backed run only see coarse routing/final/tool/compaction events. A DDx caller currently prints a bead title and then appears idle while the model thinks, completes an LLM turn, runs tools, sends the final response, or compacts context. The desired stdout lines are examples like "thinking ...", "thinking complete 8 tok in 5s", "running tool call `ls -al` ...", "completed in 3s, 100 tok", "sending response 100 tok", plus a running session-context summary.

ROOT CAUSE
  service_execute.go:750-818 bridges internal/core callback events to the public harness/service event stream, but forwards only EventToolCall as tool_call/tool_result and EventCompactionEnd as compaction. internal/core/loop.go:357-512 emits EventLLMRequest/EventLLMResponse with per-turn usage and latency, internal/core/stream_consume.go:139 emits EventLLMDelta for streaming deltas, and internal/core/loop.go:247-279 emits compaction start/end with token counts, but fizeau does not expose a stable public progress event that summarizes turn state or current session context.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Extend internal/harnesses/types.go and service_events.go with a new CONTRACT-003-compatible event type, e.g. progress, and a ServiceProgressData/HarnessProgressData payload with fields: phase (thinking|tool|response|context|compaction), state (start|complete|update), message, turn_index, tool_name, command, duration_ms, input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens, context_messages, context_tokens_estimate, session_summary.
  - In service_execute.go native callback bridge, translate EventLLMRequest into progress phase=thinking state=start, EventLLMResponse into phase=thinking state=complete with usage/latency, EventToolCall into phase=tool start+complete summaries, EventCompactionEnd into both compaction and context progress, and final response into phase=response state=complete.
  - Include bounded context summary text derived from compaction summaries when available and otherwise a compact rolling summary of turn/tool counts and latest tool names; never include full prompt, full tool output, secrets, or unbounded transcript text.
  - Persist the progress events into the service session log through the existing serviceSessionLog path just like other public events.

NON-SCOPE
  - DDx CLI rendering of the progress event; tracked downstream in the DDx repo.
  - Subprocess harness parity beyond passing through any progress events they already emit; a follow-up can teach claude/codex/gemini subprocess adapters to synthesize the same progress contract.
  - Changing final billing/cost accounting.

PARENT
  No parent.

DEPS
  No deps.
notes: Reset spurious retry-loop failure from Claude quota exhaustion; verified landed implementation commit e4db9fb plus tagged progress-test seam.
title: service-events: emit bounded progress updates for LLM turns, tools, responses, and context

description:
## Benchmark Divergence: cobol-modernization

**Sonnet 4.6 (openrouter):** PASS (reward=1.0, run: smoke-cobol-modernization-20260502T202221Z)
**Vidar (Qwen3.5-27B-4bit, omlx):** FAIL (reward=0.0, NonZeroAgentExitCodeError, run: smoke-cobol-modernization-20260502T202731Z)

### Vidar failure log (tail of fiz.txt)
```
2026/05/02 20:32:05 WARN tool-call loop: identical calls repeated 3 times, aborting
{
  "status": "failed",
  "error": "agent: identical tool calls repeated, aborting loop",
  "tokens": { "input": 109409, "output": 2334, "total": 111743 },
  "model": "Qwen3.5-27B-4bit"
}
```

### Hypothesis
Qwen3.5-27B-4bit got stuck in a repetitive tool call loop (same tool called 3x identically), triggering the loop-detection abort. This is a known failure mode for smaller/quantized Qwen models — they can get stuck re-issuing the same command without processing results, possibly because the context gets too large (109k input tokens) and the model loses track of what it's done.
title: bench/cobol-modernization: sonnet pass, vidar tool-call loop

Implement normalized utilization tracking for the existing oMLX provider type. oMLX is an OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible Apple Silicon server with default Fizeau URL http://localhost:1235/v1. It exposes serving stats through `/api/status` and richer admin stats through `/admin/api/stats`; request usage also supports `stream_options.include_usage`.

acceptance:
1. Add or update TestSessionContextCancelKillsProcessGroup in internal/pty/session/session_unix_test.go to start `sh -c 'sleep 300 & echo child:$!; wait'`, cancel the context, call Wait, and assert the printed child PID no longer exists.
2. Add or update TestKillCleansProcessGroup in internal/pty/session/session_unix_test.go so it proves Session.Kill terminates both the PTY shell and its background child without signalling the caller process group.
3. Add a regression assertion that the spawned PTY process has a distinct process group from the test process before kill/cancel is exercised.
4. `go test ./internal/pty/session -run 'Test(SessionContextCancelKillsProcessGroup|KillCleansProcessGroup|TimeoutCancelCleanupAndEOF)' -count=1` passes.
5. `go test ./...` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Subprocess harness sessions can leave orphaned Codex/DDx-style child processes when a cancellation, timeout, or direct Kill path terminates only the immediate PTY command. Those orphaned children reparent to PID 1 and continue consuming CPU, memory, and session quota.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
internal/pty/session/session_unix.go:42 starts the PTY command with exec.Command but does not assign the command to a Fizeau-owned process group before pty.StartWithSize. internal/pty/session/session_unix.go:121-129 then attempts to kill the command's process group, but without a dedicated group this is unreliable and can target the caller's process group rather than just the harness tree. internal/pty/session/session_unix_test.go:113 already covers a shell that starts a background child, so the test surface exists but the process-group ownership contract is not explicit enough.

PROPOSED FIX
On Unix, start PTY commands in a dedicated process group/session compatible with the PTY setup, and make context cancellation, WithTimeout, and Session.Kill terminate that owned process group. Prefer a TERM then short KILL escalation if the local pattern supports it; at minimum ensure the final KILL is scoped to the Fizeau-owned group. Preserve existing PTY output, resize, event, and ExitStatus behavior.

NON-SCOPE
No Windows implementation beyond preserving existing build tags. No provider routing, catalog, or DDx tracker behavior changes outside this bead.

PARENT AND DEPENDENCIES
Parent: none. Depends on: none.
notes: Reset spurious retry-loop failure from Claude quota exhaustion; verified landed implementation commit 543f7cb with acceptance checks.
title: pty-session: kill harness process groups on cancel and timeout

description:
Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit (vidar, low reasoning) has hit ErrToolCallLoop on multiple tasks.

OBSERVED FAILURES

1. build-cython-ext (034311Z run, first vidar run):
   - Error: agent: identical tool calls repeated, aborting loop
   - Tokens: 400845 in / 5575 out
   - Job: benchmark-results/harbor-jobs/build-cython-ext-20260503T025759Z

2. break-filter-js-from-html (034311Z run):
   - Error: agent: identical tool calls repeated, aborting loop
   - Tokens: 38305 in / 13211 out (11 total tool calls before loop)
   - Repeated call: {"command": "mkdir -p /tests && cp /app/filter.py /tests/filter.py"} (3 consecutive times)
   - Job: benchmark-results/harbor-jobs/break-filter-js-from-html-20260503T034311Z
   - Session: .../sessions/*.jsonl has 11797 events

ROOT CAUSE
After the repeated command (mkdir/cp) executed once, the model stopped processing the result and
just issued the same command again. With 'low' reasoning budget, Qwen3 has very few think tokens
per step and appears to not reflect on tool outputs before generating the next tool call.

INVESTIGATION NEEDED
1. Read the session JSONL around events 11700-11797 to see the exact LLM response that produced
   the 3 repeated calls — was it the same response replayed, or 3 separate responses each producing
   the same call?
2. Compare with a 'medium' reasoning run to see if more think tokens prevents the loop.
3. Consider improving the AGENTS.md/system prompt to add explicit instruction:
   'Before repeating a tool call, check if the previous call succeeded and why you are repeating it.'

DO NOT MERGE any prompt changes without verifying they help at least one of these tasks pass.
title: benchmark: Qwen3 (vidar) repeatedly hits ErrToolCallLoop — investigate reasoning budget and prompt

description:
benchmark: bragi runs produce 0 tokens on all tasks

ROOT CAUSE FOUND AND FIXED (2026-05-03):
Harbor task Docker Compose networks use isolated bridge DNS that does NOT
inherit the host's Tailscale DNS. When fiz inside the task container tries
to connect to http://bragi:1234/v1, DNS resolution for 'bragi' fails with
"Could not resolve host". This causes fiz to hang at the first API call and
produce 0 output until harbor's agent timeout kills the process.

The same issue affects vidar (http://vidar:1235/v1) — confirmed via docker
network tests on named Compose networks. Earlier vidar runs only worked
because the default Docker bridge network WAS used (before isolating into
named per-task networks, or via a different docker run invocation).

Additional complexity with bragi/LMStudio: even accessing by Tailscale IP
(100.127.38.115) fails because LMStudio uses Host-header routing and returns
404 when the Host header is an IP address instead of the hostname 'bragi'.

FIX: harbor_agent.py now resolves FIZEAU_BASE_URL hostname on the host
(where Tailscale DNS works) during container install and injects the
{hostname → ip} mapping into /etc/hosts. This provides both DNS resolution
AND correct Host header in HTTP requests.

Fix committed: 69eb184 "fix: inject FIZEAU_BASE_URL hostname into /etc/hosts
for harbor task containers"

Closing this bead — root cause resolved.
title: benchmark: bragi runs produce 0 tokens on all tasks — check Docker→Tailscale network or response latency

Fizeau ServiceConfig no longer requires model-route methods. 3. RouteStatus no longer enumerates configured model routes; it exposes live provider/model routing status or is replaced by a non-route-table status API. 4. DDx can implement its endpoint ServiceConfig without no-op ModelRoute* stubs after upgrading to the tagged release. 5. go test ./... passes.

Current Fizeau v0.10.7 still requires ServiceConfig implementations to define ModelRouteNames, ModelRouteCandidates, ModelRouteConfig, ServiceModelRouteConfig, and ServiceRouteCandidateEntry. Downstream DDx is forced to carry no-op compatibility stubs solely because those methods remain in the interface. Remove this legacy contract surface and any RouteStatus behavior that enumerates configured model routes. Route/status surfaces should expose live providers, presented models, quota, usage, cost, power, and ResolveRoute previews, not user-authored route tables. In-scope files: service.go, service_routestatus.go, service_models.go, internal/config legacy model route files/tests, cmd/bench/configadapter.go, affected tests/docs. Out-of-scope: changing Execute routing semantics except where needed to remove model_routes shortcuts.

Verified acceptance grep leaves only ADR historical references; go test ./... passes.
title: Remove model route methods from Fizeau ServiceConfig

acceptance:
1. TestExecute_EmitsRouteProgressBeforeHarnessWork verifies Execute emits a progress event before the first harness/model work event and that the progress message includes harness, provider, model, predicted power, predicted speed, and predicted cost/cost_source when present.
2. TestProgressMessages_BoundedUnder80Chars verifies every operator-facing progress Message and SessionSummary produced by route, thinking, tool, response, context, and compaction paths is <= 80 characters.
3. TestNativeProgress_ModelTurnsHaveShortSummaries verifies native LLM request/response events produce per-turn thinking start/complete progress with token and duration metadata plus a bounded short summary.
4. TestSubprocessProgress_ModelTurnsHaveShortSummaries verifies subprocess harness progress includes per-turn model request/response summaries, not just tool/final progress.
5. Existing routing_decision and final structured events remain present and unchanged except for additive fields if needed.
6. go test ./... green.
description:
PROBLEM
DDx operators need the fizeau event stream to make long-running work understandable immediately. The first visible output should include the selected route's harness/provider/model plus predicted power, speed, and cost before model execution starts, not only after final usage is known. Each major model back-and-forth should also emit a short progress summary line under 80 characters so downstream CLIs can display "thinking", tool, response, context, and compaction progress without inventing their own summaries.

ROOT CAUSE
service_execute.go:432-448 emits routing_decision at start with selected route and candidates, but it is a routing event, not a concise progress message that downstream human-output tailers can render uniformly. service_progress.go:95-130 emits native thinking start/complete progress and service_execute.go:1305-1324 emits subprocess tool/final progress, but subprocess model request/response turns are not summarized as per-turn progress messages. service_progress.go:324-345 builds session summaries with a 240-rune bound, and service_progress.go:534-543 only provides a generic max-rune helper, so there is no contract that operator-facing progress Message or SessionSummary strings stay below 80 characters.

PROPOSED FIX
- Emit a start-of-run progress event immediately after route selection and before harness execution. The message must summarize selected harness/provider/model and predicted power, speed_tps, cost_usd_per_1k_tokens, and cost_source when available, all bounded to <80 characters.
- Ensure major model back-and-forth emits progress events for both native and subprocess harnesses: request/thinking start, response/thinking complete with token/duration data, tool start/complete, response send/complete, context summary updates, and compaction events.
- Add a single summary-shortening helper used by progress Message and SessionSummary fields so downstream tools can rely on <80-character operator lines.
- Preserve existing structured routing_decision and final events; this is an additive event-stream/session-log improvement.

NON-SCOPE
- DDx CLI rendering changes; DDx should consume these fizeau events separately.
- Changing route scoring or provider/model selection.
- Emitting raw prompts, raw tool outputs, or unbounded transcript text.

PARENT
No parent.

DEPS
No deps.
notes: Re-triaged after SD-011 canonical progress event design. This bead remains the operator-facing short-summary requirement, but implementation must be through the SD-011 canonical progress schema/sink and shared summarization helpers, not local service_progress.go-only heuristics or DDx formatter special cases. It depends on fizeau-39d27326 and fizeau-f7fe4998.
title: progress: emit start route economics and short model-turn summaries

acceptance: 1. Operator config knob providers.<name>.daily_token_budget added to config schema (optional; absent => no local prediction for that provider). 2. Rolling-window per-provider token usage tracker (request + response tokens) keyed by provider name. 3. When burn-rate * remaining_window > remaining_budget, provider transitions to quota_exhausted with retry_after = next budget-window reset. 4. Reset timing semantics documented (UTC daily by default; configurable later). 5. Unit tests cover: under-budget no-transition, predicted-exhaustion transition, window reset clearing usage. 6. Existing tests pass. 7. Depends on fizeau-92b4b823.
description: Maintain a rolling per-provider token-usage window in fizeau and compare against an operator-configurable daily token budget (providers.<name>.daily_token_budget: e.g. 1_000_000) defined in ~/.config/agent/config.yaml. When local burn-rate predicts the budget will be exhausted before the next reset, transition the provider to quota_exhausted (using the state machine from fizeau-92b4b823) without waiting for the actual quota error. Multi-account model is locked: each API key is its own provider entry (e.g., claude-acct-1, claude-acct-2); fizeau treats them independently — no schema for multi-key-per-provider, just reuse existing provider abstraction. This bead is the local-signal half of prediction; the header-driven half lives in fizeau-50b14297. The two signals OR together — whichever predicts exhaustion first wins.
title: fizeau: per-provider burn-rate tracker + daily_token_budget config + predictive take-out

description:
PROBLEM
Long-running Fizeau or DDx processes need a startup reconciliation pass for stale subprocess harnesses left behind by prior crashes, OOM/no-space failures, terminal disconnects, or older cleanup bugs. Without a reaper, old `codex --no-alt-screen` and similar harness children can pile up across service restarts.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
service.go:823-862 constructs the service, starts quota refresh/recovery workers, and returns without reconciling previously-owned subprocess sessions. service_execute.go:89-195 opens a new service session and launches runExecute, while service_execute.go:1220-1252 delegates subprocess work to harness runners using the request context only. There is no durable owned-process registry or startup cleanup path tying stale subprocess PIDs back to Fizeau before new work begins.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce a narrow Fizeau-owned subprocess session registry or pidfile/log record for subprocess harness launches, including session ID, harness, command, PID, process group, started_at, and terminal state. During New or the first subprocess Execute, scan only those owned records, ignore live/current records inside a grace window, remove dead entries, and terminate stale owned process groups. Emit a diagnostic/session-log event for any reaped process group.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement broad `pkill codex` behavior or kill unowned user processes. Do not change routing selection or quota policy. Do not clean historical files unrelated to Fizeau-owned subprocess records.

PARENT AND DEPENDENCIES
Parent: none. Depends on: process-group ownership from the PTY cleanup bead.
title: service: reap stale harness sessions before starting new work

## Benchmark Issue: regex-log

**Sonnet 4.6:** FAIL (reward=0.0, TLS cert error, run: smoke-regex-log-20260503T002328Z)

### Failure
```
tls: failed to verify certificate: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority
tokens: 0 in / 0 out
```

### Root cause
Same as overfull-hbox — the Docker container for this task lacks proper CA certificates. The fiz binary inside the container cannot verify OpenRouter's TLS certificate. This is a task image configuration issue.
title: bench/regex-log: TLS cert error in container (same as overfull-hbox)

acceptance:
1. SummarizeToolCall, SummarizeOutput, throughput formatting, and redaction helpers live in internal/transcript or equivalent, not in individual wrappers.
2. Tests cover sed, rg/search, git, test commands, long paths including session_log_format.go, long output, and sensitive output redaction.
3. Tests prove important basenames survive path compaction and no arbitrary sub-40-character display limit is enforced.
4. service_progress.go uses the shared helpers instead of local duplicate semantics.
5. go test ./... passes.
description:
Move action, target, output excerpt, byte/line count, timing, and throughput summarization into the Fizeau transcript/progress package defined by SD-011. The purpose is to keep semantic interpretation out of provider wrappers and downstream DDx formatters.

In-scope files:
- internal/transcript summarization/redaction helpers
- service_progress.go call sites using those helpers
- table-driven tests for shell and tool summaries

Required samples:
- sed line ranges such as sed -n 240p and sed -n 200,260p
- rg/find style searches
- git log/show/diff/status commands
- test commands
- long paths including cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go
- long output with bytes/lines/excerpt
- sensitive-looking output that must be redacted before excerpting

Out-of-scope:
- Harness-wide migration; covered by fizeau-65174e92.
- DDx formatter changes; tracked upstream.
title: Extract progress summarization and redaction helpers

acceptance:

2. Active/queued counts, max concurrency, cache pressure when known, freshness, source, observed_at, and server_instance are normalized consistently.
3. Cassettes/goldens cover the parser behavior for each implemented local provider probe.
4. Failed/stale probes fall back to fizeau_leases with unknown provider utilization and keep otherwise healthy endpoints eligible.

acceptance:
1. Candidate ScoreComponents include power, cost, deployment/locality, quota/health, utilization, performance, context headroom, and sticky affinity where applicable.
2. Unknown utilization/performance is represented deliberately and tested so it cannot beat known healthy evidence by accident.
3. Hard saturation can reject or heavily penalize a candidate; mild load only affects ranking.
4. Statement-backed tests prove severe pressure can beat sticky affinity while a merely better score does not churn an existing sticky server.
5. go test ./internal/routing ./cmd/fiz -run "Score|Utilization|Performance|Context|Sticky" passes.
description: Add score components for normalized utilization, performance/latency/throughput, context headroom, and sticky server-instance affinity. Unknown signals should carry a mild explicit penalty or neutral value according to policy, not an accidental zero that dominates routing.
title: Score utilization performance context and stickiness

acceptance:
1. A beadbench report fixture imports into benchmark-evidence/v1 JSONL with one record per task/arm/run where available.
2. Imported records preserve bead id, arm id, model/harness/provider, task category/capability, execution outcome, runtime/cost/tool-call fields when present, and session/evidence artifact references.
3. Setup/auth/provider invalid outcomes are represented with invalid_class and denominator metadata when identifiable.

description:
beadbench is the product-native benchmark for DDx/Fizeau execute-bead work. Its reports should become first-class evidence records so FHI reflects real implementation-loop behavior, not only external benchmark scores.

In-scope files:
- scripts/beadbench/run_beadbench.py report schema/metadata if needed
- cmd/bench evidence import-beadbench command or equivalent importer
- fixtures under scripts/beadbench or cmd/bench testdata

Out-of-scope:
- changing beadbench task selection or running paid beadbench jobs
- importing TerminalBench matrices
- FHI formula or claim generation

Implementation notes:
- Import per-task/per-arm records from beadbench report.json.
- Preserve DDx bead id, project/repo, harness/model/provider arm, Fizeau version if present, execution outcome, review outcome if present, runtime/cost/tool-call fields when present, and session/evidence artifact paths/hashes.
- Distinguish implementation failures from setup/auth/provider invalids where the report has enough signal.

description:
## Benchmark Divergence: break-filter-js-from-html

**Sonnet 4.6 (openrouter):** PASS (reward=1.0, run: smoke-break-filter-js-from-html-20260502T225046Z)
**Vidar (Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit, omlx):** FAIL (reward=0.0, NonZeroAgentExitCodeError, run: smoke-break-filter-js-from-html-20260502T225839Z)

### Sonnet session summary
Model solved the XSS filter bypass by exploiting BeautifulSoup vs. Chrome HTML5 parser discrepancy. Used payload: <svg><style><script>alert(1)</script></style></svg>. Cost: $2.41, tokens: 714930 in / 17745 out.

### Vidar failure
reasoning stall: model produced only reasoning tokens past stall timeout (model=Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit, timeout=5m0s). Tokens: 2738 in / 141 out.

### Hypothesis
Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit entered an extended chain-of-thought reasoning loop without producing any tool calls or output tokens, triggering the 5-minute stall timeout. The model likely got stuck exploring the XSS bypass problem internally without taking action. This is a known pattern with Qwen3 thinking models on problems requiring iterative experimentation.
title: bench/break-filter-js-from-html: sonnet pass, vidar reasoning stall

acceptance:

SD-012 captures external benchmark resources for Rapid-MLX MHI, SkillsBench, SWE-bench, and HumanEval. Fizeau needs importer support for curated external rows/reports so those sources can contribute to model power and FHI without pretending every source is a live Fizeau run.

Out-of-scope:

- scraping sites in normal tests

Implementation notes:
- Prefer file-based import of curated snapshots over live network scraping.
- Every imported row must record source URL, capture date, benchmark version, model_raw, harness/provider when known or `unknown` when hidden, score metric, score value, and source artifact hash.
- HumanEval should be marked as low-cost raw coding/model-power evidence, not primary harness-intelligence evidence.
title: Import external benchmark resources into evidence ledger

acceptance: 1. go test ./internal/routing ./... -run 'Utilization|Sticky|RouteStatus|Routing' passes. 2. New sticky keys distribute across equivalent endpoints by normalized load. 3. Existing sticky keys stay pinned even if another endpoint later becomes less loaded. 4. A saturated endpoint is avoided for new sticky keys but does not move existing keys unless hard-saturation policy requires it. 5. Stale or missing provider utilization falls back to Fizeau lease counts. 6. Hard provider/model/harness pins and MinPower/MaxPower behavior are unchanged.
description: Wire normalized endpoint utilization and in-process route leases into routing decisions for equivalent local endpoints. In-scope files: service_routing.go, internal/routing engine inputs/scoring, routehealth/service utilization aggregation, and routing tests. Requirements: existing sticky key wins when valid; new sticky keys choose least-loaded equivalent local/free endpoint serving the resolved model; load combines Fizeau in-flight lease counts with fresh provider utilization; saturated endpoints are avoided for new keys; stale utilization falls back to lease counts; utilization only affects ranking among equivalent eligible local endpoints and must not override hard pins, power bounds, model quality, or capability gates. Out of scope: provider probe implementation, route-status rendering, session artifact rendering, and shared multi-machine lease backend.
title: Route new sticky keys by endpoint load

acceptance: Replaying a historical session log whose session.end data contains `cost_usd` = -1 renders 'Cost: unknown'; replay still renders known positive costs and explicit zero-cost local runs correctly; regression coverage includes a legacy log fixture or equivalent hand-written JSONL input.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: session/replay.go:108
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new pointer-based `SessionEndData.CostUSD` handling only treats `nil` as unknown. Historical `session.end` logs produced before this commit encoded unknown cost as `cost_usd: -1`. When those logs are replayed now, `Replay` falls through the non-positive branch and renders `Cost: $0 (local)` instead of `Cost: unknown`, which misclassifies existing session history and breaks the forward-compatibility expectation for replaying older JSONL logs.
Suggested fix: Preserve compatibility for historical session logs by treating negative `cost_usd` values as unknown during replay (and ideally add regression coverage with a hand-written legacy log fixture containing `cost_usd: -1`).
title: integration: preserve legacy unknown-cost replay semantics

acceptance: When Run executes with a StreamingProvider and NoStream=false, the successful llm.response event includes attempt metadata with provider_name, provider_system, requested_model, response_model/resolved_model, and cost source; streaming and non-streaming paths both pass regression tests covering metadata propagation.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit de8c772.
File: loop.go:162, loop.go:210, stream_consume.go:27, provider/openai/openai.go:184, provider/anthropic/anthropic.go:180
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: The new retry and attempt metadata only survives the non-streaming path. Run still prefers consumeStream whenever a provider implements StreamingProvider, which both OpenAI and Anthropic do. consumeStream assembles only content, tool calls, usage, model, and finish reason; it has no way to carry AttemptMetadata. The loop then backfills only AttemptIndex when resp.Attempt is nil. As a result, the default execution path loses ProviderName, ProviderSystem, RequestedModel, ResponseModel, ResolvedModel, and cost provenance even though this commit's acceptance requires runtime attempt metadata sufficient for provider identity and attribution. Current tests cover only non-streaming metadata population, so this regression is not caught.
Suggested fix: Extend the streaming path so provider metadata is emitted and preserved through StreamDelta and consumeStream, and add regression tests that run Run with streaming-enabled providers and assert the emitted llm.response attempt payload includes provider, model, and cost metadata on success.
title: correctness: streaming runs drop attempt metadata required by runtime retries

acceptance: Streaming timing uses real delta arrival timestamps rather than subtracting cumulative callback time; a regression test with callback sleep plus inter-delta provider delay verifies ddx.timing.generation_ms still includes the provider delay and EventLLMDelta timestamps remain monotonic.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: stream_consume.go:44
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: consumeStream now computes each delta timestamp as time.Now().Add(-callbackDelay) and grows callbackDelay by the duration of prior EventLLMDelta callbacks. When a later streamed delta becomes ready while an earlier callback is still running, this subtracts both observer latency and real provider generation time that elapsed during that callback. In the shipped providers, ChatStream uses a single-slot channel, so later deltas can queue behind the consumer and then be back-dated when read. That makes ddx.timing.generation_ms and delta event timestamps too small or even flat across distinct arrivals, which violates CONTRACT-001's requirement that timing reflect the streamed output window rather than an adjusted observer schedule.
Suggested fix: Capture an arrival timestamp at the producer or carry it on StreamDelta so consumeStream can use actual delta arrival times. At minimum, add a regression test that combines callback sleep with real delayBetween timing and verifies generation_ms still includes the provider delay that elapsed while the callback was running.
title: correctness: callback-delay compensation undercounts real stream generation

acceptance: HOME set to a fresh temp directory and running 'go run ./cmd/ddx-agent update --check-only' no longer fails because ~/.cache/agent is missing; cmd/ddx-agent has a regression test covering GetLatestRelease or cmdUpdate with an absent cache directory
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cmd/ddx-agent/update.go:138
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: GetLatestRelease now returns any cache-write error from saveCachedVersion. cmdUpdate and cmdVersion pass ~/.cache/agent/latest-version.json without creating ~/.cache/agent first, so a first-run update check fails after a successful GitHub response with 'open .../.cache/agent/latest-version.json: no such file or directory'. Reproduced with: HOME=/tmp/tmp.guOtfw5Bpt go run ./cmd/ddx-agent update --check-only
Suggested fix: Treat cache persistence as best-effort or create the parent cache directory before writing, and add a regression test that covers a missing cache directory.
title: integration: update check fails on first run when cache directory is absent

acceptance: For Anthropic streaming runs, token usage from message_start and message_delta is merged into the final Response and llm.response event; Input, Output, CacheRead, and CacheWrite are preserved; Total equals Input+Output; a regression test covers split usage deltas
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 51f778b.
File: provider/anthropic/anthropic.go:236, provider/anthropic/anthropic.go:275, stream_consume.go:84
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Anthropic streaming emits input tokens on the initial message_start delta and output/cache tokens on later message_delta events, but consumeStream replaces resp.Usage on each delta instead of accumulating it. In the default streaming path, the final Response therefore drops the earlier input token counts and retains only the last usage fragment. Anthropic's streaming code also never sets Total, so even if the fragments were merged the run-level total token count would remain zero. This leaves llm.response, session.end, and any cost/accounting derived from streaming Anthropic responses inconsistent with the non-streaming provider path.
Suggested fix: Accumulate streaming usage fragments in consumeStream instead of overwriting them, and have the Anthropic streaming path emit enough data to compute a correct final TokenUsage.Total, or emit the full merged usage on the terminal delta. Add a regression test that simulates Anthropic-style split usage deltas and asserts the final Response and emitted response event preserve input, output, cache, and total token counts.
title: correctness: anthropic streaming usage loses input tokens across deltas

acceptance: A session whose chat spans all have known cost preserves a root invoke_agent ddx.cost.amount equal to the sum of child costs even when provenance differs across turns; the root span only emits ddx.cost.source=unknown when at least one contributing chat span has unknown cost; automated tests cover mixed provider-reported/configured cost rollups.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: loop.go:289
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new root-span cost rollup logic marks the invoke_agent span as unknown whenever known child chat spans disagree on ddx.cost.source, currency, or pricing_ref. That drops a valid total for mixed but fully known sessions, which violates CONTRACT-001's rule that the root cost only becomes unknown when a contributing chat span has unknown cost. Reproduced against the current code by running a two-turn session with a provider-reported first turn and an exact configured-cost second turn: result.CostUSD=0.03 while the root span still emits ddx.cost.source=unknown.
Suggested fix: Roll up root ddx.cost.amount whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only force ddx.cost.source=unknown when a contributing span is actually unknown. If metadata such as pricing_ref or source cannot be represented cleanly at the root, omit or summarize those fields rather than discarding the known total. Add a regression test covering mixed known-cost provenance across turns.
title: correctness: preserve known root cost rollups across mixed known cost sources

acceptance: A ddx-agent run records provider, model, and work_dir on the session.start event; ddx-agent usage --json reports the configured provider/model for that session instead of provider=unknown; ddx-agent replay <session-id> shows the configured provider and working directory; regression coverage asserts those values in the cross-cwd relative-session-log test.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: loop.go:45
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The reviewed fix correctly routes session log files through workDir, but agent.Run still emits session.start without the provider, model, or work_dir fields required by FEAT-005. As a result, ddx-agent usage aggregates these sessions under provider=unknown, replay cannot show the configured provider or working directory, and the new functional regression test in cmd/ddx-agent/catalog_cli_test.go now codifies that broken behavior by asserting provider == "unknown" instead of catching it.
Suggested fix: Thread provider/model/workdir metadata into the session.start event payload emitted by agent.Run (or otherwise arrange for the callback logger to receive it), then update the cross-cwd CLI regression test to assert the real provider/model/work_dir values in usage and replay output.
title: correctness: session.start omits provider/model/workdir metadata

acceptance: Running ddx-agent usage with an invalid --since value exits with code 2, prints the validation error, and a functional CLI test asserts the behavior.

Suggested fix: Distinguish usage/config validation failures from runtime failures in cmdUsage and return 2 for invalid --since and other config/usage errors; add a CLI test that asserts the exit code.

acceptance: When a provider implements SessionStartMetadata but not ChatStartMetadata and Chat returns an error, the ended chat span records ddx.provider.system with that provider identity instead of unknown, while OpenAI-compatible providers still record resolved server.address and server.port.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: loop.go:182
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new chat-span bootstrap path always sources ddx.provider.system from chatStartIdentity. When a provider does not implement ChatStartMetadata, chatStartIdentity returns "unknown" instead of preserving the existing session-level provider identity. Before this commit, StartChat seeded ProviderSystem from sessionProvider, so failed calls from providers that only implement SessionStartMetadata still retained a meaningful identity. This change regresses those error spans to "unknown". The repo already has such a provider in provider/virtual/virtual.go, which exposes SessionStartMetadata but not ChatStartMetadata.
Suggested fix: Make chatStartIdentity fall back to sessionStartIdentity(provider) for providerSystem when ChatStartMetadata is unavailable, while still leaving server address and port unset unless the richer metadata hook is implemented.
title: integration: preserve provider identity fallback for providers without ChatStartMetadata

acceptance: Compactor emits EventCompactionStart before compaction

The loop emits EventCompactionEnd on both success and error paths, but no start event. SD-006 defines both event types, implying both should be emitted. Emit start before calling req.Compactor.

title: Fix: EventCompactionStart never emitted in agent loop

acceptance: A JSON payload using the DDx envelope field `title` but missing required `id` and `prompt` fails with an invalid prompt envelope error for -p, stdin, and @file input paths, while non-envelope JSON objects like `{"kind":"note","text":"hello"}` still pass through as raw prompt text; regression tests cover both behaviors.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cmd/ddx-agent/main.go:381
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `isPromptEnvelopeProbe` now treats `kind` plus `prompt`, `id`, `inputs`, `response_schema`, or `callback` as an envelope candidate, but it still ignores the optional `title` field even though `ddxPromptEnvelope` accepts and emits it. A malformed DDx payload like `{"kind":"prompt","title":"Inspect main"}` is therefore treated as raw prompt text instead of failing validation, so execution falls through to provider selection on all entry paths. Repro: `go run ./cmd/ddx-agent -p '{"kind":"prompt","title":"Inspect main"}' -provider nonexistent` returns `config: unknown provider "nonexistent"` instead of `invalid prompt envelope`; the same happens for stdin and `@file` because all three routes call `resolvePrompt` -> `parsePromptInput`.\nSuggested fix: include `title` in the probe heuristic for recognized envelope fields, and add regression coverage showing that `kind`+`title` without required `id`/`prompt` fails for inline, stdin, and `@file` inputs while non-envelope JSON such as `{"kind":"note","text":"hello"}` still passes through as raw prompt text.
title: correctness: reject malformed prompt envelopes with only title

acceptance: Network error during streaming causes consumeStream to return a non-nil error; partial content is not treated as a successful response; tests cover error propagation from both OpenAI and Anthropic providers
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: provider/openai/openai.go:213-244, provider/anthropic/anthropic.go:219-271
Category: bug
Severity: high
Description: Both ChatStream goroutines iterate stream.Next() and then unconditionally send Done:true. If stream.Next() returns false due to a network error, stream.Err() is never checked. The goroutine sends Done:true and the channel closes normally. consumeStream returns a partial Response with no error, and the agent loop treats it as a successful response. This silently corrupts tool call arguments or truncates content.
Suggested fix: After the stream loop exits, check stream.Err(). If non-nil, send a StreamDelta with an embedded error signal OR close the channel and handle via a separate error channel. Simplest approach: add an Error field to StreamDelta, check it in consumeStream, and return the error.
title: bug: stream errors swallowed in OpenAI and Anthropic ChatStream

acceptance: A JSON payload intended as a DDx prompt envelope but missing the required id field fails with an invalid prompt envelope error for -p, stdin, and @file input paths, while non-envelope JSON objects still pass through as raw prompt text; regression tests cover both behaviors.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cmd/ddx-agent/main.go:331
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new probe heuristic only treats JSON as a DDx prompt envelope when `kind` is paired with `id`, `inputs`, `response_schema`, or `callback`. A malformed envelope like {"kind":"prompt","prompt":"Read main.go"} is therefore treated as raw prompt text instead of failing validation, so execution proceeds to provider selection. This violates the structured-I/O contract in the PRD and leaves invalid envelope input non-deterministic. Repro: `go run ./cmd/ddx-agent -p '{"kind":"prompt","prompt":"Read main.go"}' -provider nonexistent` returns `config: unknown provider "nonexistent"` instead of `invalid prompt envelope`.\nSuggested fix: Treat objects that contain `kind` plus `prompt` as envelope candidates too, so missing required `id` still reaches envelope validation; add regression coverage for missing-id envelopes across inline, stdin, and @file inputs.
title: correctness: reject malformed prompt envelopes missing id

acceptance: If attempt cost provenance is unknown, llm.response and session.end do not emit a positive cost_usd derived from DefaultPricing; known provider-reported or configured costs still propagate; regression tests cover known-cost and unknown-cost cases.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit de8c772.
File: loop.go:219, loop.go:241, provider/openai/openai.go:83, provider/anthropic/anthropic.go:90, pricing.go:12
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: This change introduces structured cost provenance on resp.Attempt.Cost and explicitly marks both providers as source=unknown, but the runtime still unconditionally computes cost_usd from DefaultPricing and emits it on every successful llm.response. That produces internally contradictory telemetry: the same event can say the attempt cost source is unknown while also publishing a numeric USD value from a static fallback table. CONTRACT-001 explicitly forbids generic stale pricing tables as a fallback and requires unknown cost to remain unknown when no reported or configured cost exists.
Suggested fix: Stop deriving per-attempt and session cost from DefaultPricing once attempt-level cost provenance exists. Use provider-reported or configured runtime pricing only when available, otherwise omit numeric cost and keep the rollup unknown. Add tests that assert unknown-cost attempts do not emit positive cost_usd values.
title: correctness: llm.response cost_usd conflicts with unknown attempt cost provenance

acceptance: GlobTool and GrepTool WalkDir skip .git/ directories; unit test confirms .git contents are excluded from results
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: tool/glob.go and tool/grep.go (WalkDir callbacks)
Category: performance
Severity: medium
Description: GlobTool and GrepTool walk the entire directory tree including .git/, node_modules/, vendor/, and other commonly-ignored directories. This produces noisy results, exposes git internals in output, and causes significant slowdowns on repos with large dependency trees or deep .git/objects/ directories. Established coding tools universally skip .git at minimum.
Suggested fix: Add a directory skip list (at minimum .git) in the WalkDir callback. Consider also skipping node_modules, vendor, .hg, .svn. Optionally respect .gitignore via a library like go-gitignore.
title: performance: glob/grep WalkDir should skip .git and VCS directories

acceptance: A plain JSON prompt like {"kind":"note","title":"hello"} remains raw prompt text for -p, stdin, and @file inputs, while malformed DDx prompt envelopes like {"kind":"prompt","title":"Inspect main"} still fail with an invalid prompt envelope error; regression tests cover both behaviors.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cmd/ddx-agent/main.go:375
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `isPromptEnvelopeProbe` now treats any JSON object with top-level `kind` plus `title` as a DDx prompt envelope candidate. That fixes malformed `{"kind":"prompt","title":"..."}` envelopes, but it also re-breaks backward compatibility for plain JSON prompts whose `kind` is not the DDx envelope discriminator. Repro: `go run ./cmd/ddx-agent -p '{"kind":"note","title":"hello"}' -provider nonexistent` now returns `invalid prompt envelope: kind, id, and prompt are required` instead of passing the JSON through as raw prompt text; the same regression occurs for stdin and `@file` because all three paths call `resolvePrompt` -> `parsePromptInput`. This violates the earlier plain-JSON compatibility requirement while over-matching objects that are not DDx prompt envelopes.\nSuggested fix: narrow the probe so `title` only contributes to envelope detection when the object is actually a DDx prompt envelope candidate, for example by requiring `kind == "prompt"` before `title` triggers validation; add regression coverage proving `{"kind":"note","title":"hello"}` stays raw text for inline, stdin, and `@file`, while malformed `{"kind":"prompt","title":"Inspect main"}` still fails validation.
title: correctness: preserve non-envelope JSON with kind+title

Category: drift

Description: The last-commit change extends parsePromptInput, which is a CLI boundary parser and user-input validator for DDx prompt envelopes, but the repo still has no fuzz coverage for this path. The active testing concern requires fuzzing parsers, deserializers, and user-input validators, and searching for Go fuzz targets only finds coverage in tool/fuzz_test.go. The fuzz target should at minimum assert no panic and stable classification between raw prompt passthrough versus invalid-envelope error for malformed envelope-shaped inputs.

acceptance: With a large system prompt and a small KeepRecentTokens budget, compaction still triggers when required but the next provider call retains the active user prompt as a verbatim user message; add a regression test that fails if the final provider request contains only system prompt plus summary after compaction.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: compaction/compactor.go:48; compaction/config.go:74; loop.go:89
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The fix subtracts non-persisted system-prompt tokens directly from KeepRecentTokens and clamps the result to zero. When the system prompt is larger than the keep budget, FindCutPoint now returns len(messages), so CompactMessages summarizes the entire live history, including the current user prompt. A repro with ContextWindow=25, KeepRecentTokens=5, and an 80-character system prompt shows the final provider call receives only the system prompt plus the generated summary; the explicit user prompt  is no longer present as a real user message. SD-006 says session prefix messages are excluded from preserved user messages and shows post-compaction ordering that retains recent user messages after the system prompt. Compacting away the active prompt lets the summarizer paraphrase or omit the instruction that the next model turn is supposed to answer.
Suggested fix: Account for system-prompt tokens in the compaction trigger and post-compaction fit calculation without letting them reduce the preserved recent-conversation budget to zero. Preserve at least the last real user message/current prompt verbatim, or add cut-point logic that excludes prefix context from the keep budget while still enforcing a bounded final request size.
title: correctness: compaction can summarize away the active user prompt when prefix exhausts keep budget

That means hx-74ed3216 was closed even though one of the newly wired quality gates is not actually enforceable in CI and the workflow is now broken on every run.
Suggested fix: either pin golangci-lint to a config-compatible v1 release in CI, or migrate .golangci.yml to the v2 schema and verify make lint passes with the installed version before keeping the bead closed.

acceptance: GlobTool truncates results at a defined maximum; output includes truncation indicator; unit test verifies truncation behavior
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: tool/glob.go:57-87
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: GrepTool caps results at maxGrepResults=200 with a truncation indicator, but GlobTool has no such limit. A pattern like '**/*' on a large monorepo could return tens of thousands of paths, causing excessive memory usage and an unusably large tool response that wastes LLM context tokens.
Suggested fix: Add a maxGlobResults constant (e.g. 500) with truncation behavior matching GrepTool. Append a '(results truncated at N matches)' line when the limit is hit.
title: correctness: GlobTool needs result count limit like GrepTool

acceptance: CONTRACT-001 explicitly defines how the root invoke_agent span represents known session totals when child chat spans have mixed known provenance; the contract no longer requires an impossible or ambiguous ddx.cost.source/pricing_ref combination at the root; conformance tests or checklist coverage include a mixed provider-reported/configured session example.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-001-otel-telemetry-capture.md:221
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: CONTRACT-001 says the root invoke_agent span should emit a session cost rollup whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only become unknown when any child cost is unknown. But the contract never defines what ddx.cost.source, ddx.cost.currency, and ddx.cost.pricing_ref should look like when all child chat costs are known yet their provenance differs across turns, such as one provider-reported turn plus one configured-cost turn. That makes the required root shape ambiguous: ddx.cost.source is required when amount is known, but the allowed enum only models single-attempt provenance. The current implementation fell into this gap and downgraded a known total to unknown when provenance metadata differed.
Suggested fix: Amend the root-span cost-rollup section to specify a conformant representation for mixed known-cost provenance. Either add an explicit aggregate/root rollup source shape, or state that the root may still emit ddx.cost.amount and currency while omitting or summarizing provenance fields that cannot be represented cleanly. Add a contract-focused regression test or checklist entry covering mixed provider-reported/configured sessions.
title: correctness: define mixed-provenance root cost rollup semantics

acceptance: Streaming tool call with multi-chunk arguments assembles complete JSON; TestConsumeStream_ToolCallAssembly covers split-chunk scenario; openai ChatStream maps by index to carry ID forward for subsequent arg fragments
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: provider/openai/openai.go:226-230
Category: bug
Severity: high
Description: In ChatStream, the loop over choice.Delta.ToolCalls overwrites delta fields for each call (only last wins when multiple arrive in one chunk). Critically, OpenAI only sends tool call ID in the FIRST chunk; subsequent argument chunks have ID="" and index. consumeStream checks 'if delta.ToolCallID != ""' and ignores ID-less deltas, so all argument fragments after the initial chunk are silently dropped. Tool calls assembled from streaming will have truncated or empty arguments.
Suggested fix: Track tool calls by index (not just ID) in ChatStream. Maintain a map[int]string for known IDs so subsequent arg chunks can be dispatched with the correct ID.
title: bug: OpenAI streaming tool call arguments lost after first chunk

acceptance: When the compactor returns nil in the no-fit prefix path, Run must still emit a matching EventCompactionEnd after EventCompactionStart; add regression coverage that asserts balanced compaction start/end events for the fail-closed scenario.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: compaction/summarize.go:328; compaction/summarize.go:352; loop.go:101; loop.go:153
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new fail-closed path returns result=nil when prefix-aware compaction cannot fit. In Run, runCompaction emits EventCompactionStart before invoking the compactor, but if compResult is nil it returns at loop.go:153-154 without emitting EventCompactionEnd. AGENTS.md requires EventCompactionEnd after compaction on both success and error paths. This regression becomes reachable specifically because the reviewed commit introduces new nil-return branches for exhausted no-fit cases.
Suggested fix: emit EventCompactionEnd for the no-compaction path as well, including success=false or a dedicated no-op outcome payload, and add callback-based regression coverage for the no-fit prefix scenario so start/end event pairs remain balanced.
title: correctness: no-fit compaction path omits compaction.end event

acceptance: Streaming chat spans measure ddx.timing.first_token_ms from the same timestamp used to start the chat span; a test with a blocking EventLLMRequest callback and a streaming provider confirms first_token_ms includes callback delay before ChatStream begins.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: loop.go:175
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 4df0f7e moved `streamStart` earlier inside `consumeStream`, but `ddx.timing.first_token_ms` is still measured after the chat span starts. `Run` starts the `chat` span at loop.go:175, then emits `EventLLMRequest` before calling `consumeStream`; because callbacks are user-supplied and can block, the exported attribute can still under-report first-token latency by the callback time and therefore violate CONTRACT-001's requirement that the value measure from span start to first streamed token.
Suggested fix: Capture the chat span/request start timestamp in `Run` and thread it into `consumeStream` so `Attempt.Timing.FirstToken` is derived from the same timestamp that anchors the `chat` span. Add an end-to-end test where the request callback sleeps before streaming begins and assert the chat span's `ddx.timing.first_token_ms` includes that delay.
title: correctness: first_token_ms still excludes pre-stream callback latency

acceptance: A Run configured with both SystemPrompt and Compactor accounts for the system prompt when deciding whether to compact; a regression test demonstrates compaction still triggers when the history alone is under budget but the provider request (system prompt + history) exceeds the context limit; Result.Messages remains free of duplicated system messages.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 7e225853fcb708575f478050865d4eb9e0322c87.
File: loop.go:60; loop.go:109; loop.go:174
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new continuity refactor removes `SystemPrompt` from the persisted `messages` slice and only prepends it to `providerMessages` immediately before each provider call. `req.Compactor` still runs against `messages`, so its token estimate and cut-point decisions no longer include the system prompt even though the next provider request does. In long sessions with a large system prompt or AGENTS/developer wrapper, compaction can now decide that the history fits when the actual provider request exceeds the configured context budget. SD-006 explicitly requires the loop to keep the system prompt out of persisted history while still treating it as part of the live request prefix and context-window math. Current tests cover continuity and resumed compaction state, but none exercise compaction with a non-empty `SystemPrompt`, so this regression is not caught.
Suggested fix: Include the system prompt in compaction token accounting without persisting it into `Result.Messages`/`Request.History`, and add a regression test that runs `Run` with both `SystemPrompt` and `Compactor` enabled and verifies compaction triggers based on the full provider request size rather than the history slice alone.
title: integration: compaction ignores system prompt tokens in Run continuity path

acceptance: With ContextWindow=80, ReserveTokens=0, KeepRecentTokens=20, EffectivePercent=100, a 260-character system prompt prefix, history [120-char user, 120-char assistant, DO-THE-THING], and a mock summarizer response of x, NewCompactor must not return a non-nil result whose TokensAfter exceeds 80; add regression coverage for this exhaustion case.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 1d16a3c72774e6d4f451a036fc7a62df5c0b9bfb.
File: compaction/summarize.go:329; compaction/summarize.go:335; compaction/summarize.go:352; compaction/summarize.go:360; compaction/summarize.go:365; compaction/summarize.go:370
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new prefix-aware summary budgeting fixes the normal fit case, but when no summary can fit inside the remaining post-prefix window the exhausted-cutpoint paths still return a successful compaction result. In compactMessages, the result==nil fallback retries compactAtCutIndex with effectiveWindow=0, and the over-budget bestResult path returns the last candidate even after result.TokensAfter+prefixTokens stayed above the effective window. A direct executable repro in the current tree uses ContextWindow=80, ReserveTokens=0, KeepRecentTokens=20, EffectivePercent=100, a 260-character system prompt (estimated prefixTokens=67), history [120-char user, 120-char assistant, DO-THE-THING], and a mock summarizer response of "x". NewCompactor returns a non-nil result with TokensAfter=99, which still exceeds the 80-token effective budget.
Suggested fix: when every valid cut point still leaves the rebuilt request over budget, fail closed instead of returning an unbounded fallback or over-budget bestResult. Return nil/no compaction (or a dedicated warning result) unless the final TokensAfter including prefix fits within the effective window, and add a regression test for the 260-char prefix repro.
title: correctness: no-fit prefix-aware compaction still returns an over-budget result

acceptance: A two-run regression test that resumes from Result.Messages with a fresh compactor preserves prior summary content and file-op XML after the second compaction; long-session continuity no longer depends on reusing the same compactor closure across Run calls
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 7e225853fcb708575f478050865d4eb9e0322c87.
File: agent.go:251; loop.go:60; compaction/compactor.go:40; compaction/summarize.go:100; compaction/summarize.go:179
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new continuity API says callers can feed Result.Messages back into Request.History to continue a session, but that is not sufficient once compaction has already occurred. compaction.NewCompactor keeps previousSummary and previousFileOps in hidden closure state. On a resumed Run, only the message list is carried forward; a fresh compactor starts with empty previousSummary/previousFileOps. When compaction happens again, CompactMessages filters prior <summary> injections out of messages[:cutIndex] and passes the empty previousSummary to Summarize, so the older compacted context and accumulated file-op state can be dropped from the next summary. This makes long sessions silently lose earlier context across Run boundaries unless callers also preserve the exact compactor instance, which the new API and tests do not require or document.
Suggested fix: Make continuity durable across Run boundaries by serializing enough compaction state into the resumed history/result contract, or by teaching compaction to recover previous summary/file-op state from carried History. Add a regression test that chains two Run calls with compaction on both sides and verifies the second compaction preserves information from the first summary and its file-op XML even when the resumed run uses a fresh compactor.
title: correctness: resumed sessions lose prior compaction state on next compaction

acceptance: For the 80-token context-window repro with a 260-character system prompt prefix, agent.Run must not call Provider.Chat with provider messages whose estimated token count exceeds the effective window; add regression coverage for the no-fit compaction path at the Run integration level.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: loop.go:114; loop.go:153; loop.go:179
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: The new no-fit path in compactMessages now returns result=nil for over-budget prefix cases, but Run treats a nil compaction result as a no-op and immediately builds providerMessages from the original history. In the same repro covered by TestCompactor_SystemPromptPrefixNoFitFailsClosed, the unchanged history still estimates to 135 tokens with the system prompt prefix included (67 prefix + 31 user + 33 assistant + 4 active user), which exceeds the 80-token effective window and will still be sent to the provider. The patch therefore fixes the compactor return value but does not actually prevent an over-budget LLM request in the agent loop.
Suggested fix: when compaction was needed but the compactor cannot produce an in-budget history, fail the iteration before calling the provider or add a compactor/loop contract that surfaces an explicit over-budget outcome. Add a Run-level regression test that asserts no provider Chat call occurs with the 80-token/260-char-prefix repro unless the request fits within budget.
title: integration: no-fit prefix compaction still sends an over-budget provider request

acceptance: With a large system prompt and a small KeepRecentTokens budget, compaction still triggers when required but the next provider call retains the active user prompt as a verbatim user message; add a regression test that fails if the final provider request contains only system prompt plus summary after compaction.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: compaction/compactor.go:48; compaction/config.go:74; loop.go:89
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The fix subtracts non-persisted system-prompt tokens directly from KeepRecentTokens and clamps the result to zero. When the system prompt is larger than the keep budget, FindCutPoint now returns len(messages), so CompactMessages summarizes the entire live history, including the current user prompt. A repro with ContextWindow=25, KeepRecentTokens=5, and an 80-character system prompt shows the final provider call receives only the system prompt plus the generated summary; the explicit user prompt DO-THE-THING is no longer present as a real user message. SD-006 says session prefix messages are excluded from preserved user messages and shows post-compaction ordering that retains recent user messages after the system prompt. Compacting away the active prompt lets the summarizer paraphrase or omit the instruction that the next model turn is supposed to answer.
Suggested fix: Account for system-prompt tokens in the compaction trigger and post-compaction fit calculation without letting them reduce the preserved recent-conversation budget to zero. Preserve at least the last real user message/current prompt verbatim, or add cut-point logic that excludes prefix context from the keep budget while still enforcing a bounded final request size.
title: correctness: compaction can summarize away the active user prompt when prefix exhausts keep budget

acceptance: When an OpenAI-compatible provider with a custom BaseURL returns an error, the ended chat span still records the correct ddx.provider.system, server.address, and server.port values.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: loop.go:174
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: StartChat seeds every chat span from sessionStartIdentity, which only returns provider name and model. annotateChatSpan adds the resolved provider system and server identity only after a successful response. When Chat or consumeStream returns an error, the span ends without ddx.provider.system or server.address/server.port even though those fields are already known by the provider, so failed OpenAI-compatible requests are misattributed and lose the metadata this commit set out to capture.
Suggested fix: Extend the chat-start metadata path so StartChat receives providerSystem and server identity up front, ensuring error spans are populated without relying on a successful response annotation.
title: integration: failed chat spans lose resolved provider identity

acceptance: cmd/ddx-agent/prompt_input_fuzz_test.go no longer calls production envelope-detection helpers to decide expected classification; go test ./cmd/ddx-agent passes; go test ./cmd/ddx-agent -run=^$ -fuzz=FuzzParsePromptInput -fuzztime=2s passes
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cmd/ddx-agent/prompt_input_fuzz_test.go:39
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new fuzz target derives its expected classification by calling the production helpers isPromptEnvelopeProbe and hasPromptEnvelopeFields. Because the oracle shares the same envelope-detection logic as parsePromptInput, a regression in those helpers can affect both sides and still satisfy the fuzz assertions, leaving the main failure mode from hx-624db07d partially untested.
Suggested fix: make the fuzz oracle independent of production detection helpers, or assert invariants that do not depend on the same classification path. For example, classify malformed envelope-shaped JSON with a minimal local probe that does not call isPromptEnvelopeProbe/hasPromptEnvelopeFields, then assert parsePromptInput returns either passthrough for plain JSON or an invalid prompt-envelope error for malformed envelope-shaped inputs.
title: correctness: make prompt-input fuzz oracle independent

acceptance: go mod tidy -diff exits cleanly with no diff, and go test ./... still passes after the tidied module files are committed
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: go.mod:24
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The telemetry scaffolding commit added direct imports of OpenTelemetry modules, but go.mod still records go.opentelemetry.io/otel, go.opentelemetry.io/otel/metric, go.opentelemetry.io/otel/sdk, and go.opentelemetry.io/otel/trace as indirect dependencies. Running go mod tidy -diff produces a non-empty diff that promotes those modules to direct requirements, so the repo was not left in the concern-required tidy state after a dependency change.
Suggested fix: Run go mod tidy, commit the resulting go.mod and go.sum updates, and keep the module files clean so go mod tidy -diff is empty after the telemetry dependency addition.
title: drift: tidy telemetry module dependencies

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: session/usage.go:145
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The usage aggregator keeps summing KnownCostUSD even when a row or the grand total also contains unknown-cost sessions. ADR-001 explicitly says totals must be treated as unknown if any contributing turn is unknown, but the current implementation and tests preserve a numeric KnownCostUSD and the CLI will print a partial dollar amount, which underreports spend for mixed known/unknown aggregates.
Suggested fix: Represent aggregate cost as unknown whenever UnknownCostSessions > 0 for that row or total, update the CLI/JSON rendering to surface that state instead of a partial dollar amount, and add tests covering mixed known/unknown-cost inputs.
title: correctness: mixed unknown-cost sessions produce misleading aggregate cost totals

acceptance: go test ./... passes in a clean worktree at HEAD; TestRun_CONTRACT001MixedKnownCostProvenance observes ddx.cost.amount on the root invoke_agent span for mixed known-cost sessions; root mixed-provenance rollups omit unstable provenance metadata instead of downgrading the whole session cost to unknown.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: loop.go:696
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: Commit 5f14aaa updates CONTRACT-001 and adds TestRun_CONTRACT001MixedKnownCostProvenance, but the runtime still emits root `invoke_agent` cost as unknown when child chat spans have mixed known provenance. In a clean worktree at commit 5f14aaa, `go test ./...` fails because telemetry_contract_test.go expects the root span to carry ddx.cost.amount=0.03, while a standalone reproduction shows the ended root span still exports only ddx.cost.source=unknown. That leaves the implementation out of sync with the amended contract and keeps the original telemetry bug unfixed.
Suggested fix: Update the root-session cost rollup logic in loop.go so mixed known provenance preserves ddx.cost.amount while omitting only the unstable provenance fields (ddx.cost.source, ddx.cost.currency, ddx.cost.pricing_ref) as CONTRACT-001 now requires. Keep the new conformance test green with go test ./... in a clean worktree.
title: correctness: mixed-provenance root cost rollup still exports unknown

acceptance: When any contributing attempt has unknown cost provenance, session.end omits cost_usd instead of emitting -1; replay/session parsing distinguishes omitted cost from zero-cost local cost; regression tests cover both known-cost and unknown-cost session rollups.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: loop.go:360, loop_test.go:506, session/event.go:52, session/replay.go:108
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The last-commit fix removed DefaultPricing fallback from per-attempt telemetry, but the session rollup still serializes a numeric cost on every session.end event by copying result.CostUSD unconditionally. When any contributing attempt has unknown provenance, result.CostUSD becomes -1, so session.end now emits `cost_usd: -1` instead of omitting the amount. That still violates CONTRACT-001 and SD-001, which require unknown rollup cost amounts to be omitted. The new regression test in loop_test.go also codifies unconditional session.end cost emission, so a contract-compliant fix would currently fail tests. Downstream session consumers compound the issue because SessionEndData uses a plain float64 and Replay treats zero as `$0 (local)`, so omitted cost cannot be represented distinctly from free local execution.
Suggested fix: Make session.end omit cost_usd when the run cost is unknown, update session event/replay types to distinguish omitted cost from zero-cost local runs, and replace the current session-end assertion with regression coverage for both known-cost and unknown-cost sessions.
title: correctness: session.end still emits unknown cost as numeric sentinel

acceptance: A session whose chat spans all have known cost preserves a root invoke_agent ddx.cost.amount equal to the sum of child costs even when provenance differs across turns; the root span only emits ddx.cost.source=unknown when at least one contributing chat span has unknown cost; automated tests cover mixed provider-reported/configured cost rollups.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: loop.go:289
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The last commit's root-span cost rollup logic marks the invoke_agent span as unknown whenever known child chat spans disagree on ddx.cost.source, currency, or pricing_ref. That discards a valid session total even though every contributing chat span has known cost, which conflicts with FEAT-005 requirement 22, ADR-001's known-vs-unknown cost policy, and CONTRACT-001's rule that the root rollup only becomes unknown when a contributing chat span has unknown cost. In the committed code, a two-turn session with one provider-reported attempt and one exact configured-cost attempt yields result.CostUSD=0.03 while applySessionCostAttributes still emits root ddx.cost.source=unknown because sessionCostStable became false.
Suggested fix: Preserve root ddx.cost.amount whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only force ddx.cost.source=unknown when a contributing span is actually unknown. If source/currency/pricing_ref cannot be represented cleanly for mixed provenance, omit or summarize those metadata fields instead of discarding the known total. Add regression coverage for mixed provider-reported plus configured-cost sessions.
notes:
Superseded by commit `421f59e695319df41a2ec9016b13f652cb98ed6e` (`hx-cacc76f6 preserve mixed-provenance root cost amount`).

The root invoke_agent span now preserves `ddx.cost.amount` for mixed known-cost sessions and the regression coverage for mixed provider-reported plus configured-cost rollups is in `loop_test.go`.
title: correctness: mixed known-cost root rollups must stay known

acceptance: Streaming chat spans measure ddx.timing.first_token_ms from before ChatStream is invoked; a test with a provider whose ChatStream blocks before yielding output includes that blocking time in FirstToken.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: stream_consume.go:22
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: consumeStream records streamStart only after sp.ChatStream returns. Both provider/openai and provider/anthropic call the SDK's NewStreaming method before returning, and those SDK methods synchronously execute the HTTP request. That means ddx.timing.first_token_ms underreports time-to-first-token by excluding request setup and connection latency, so the emitted timing no longer matches CONTRACT-001's requirement to measure from request/span start.
Suggested fix: Capture the stream start timestamp before invoking sp.ChatStream, or thread the chat-span/request start timestamp into consumeStream and compute FirstToken from that value.
title: correctness: TTFT starts after ChatStream setup

acceptance: Either: (a) GlobTool and GrepTool accept an optional ExcludeDirs field that overrides skipDirs, with tests; OR (b) documented as intentional in tool schema descriptions, with a decision recorded
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: tool/glob.go:16 and tool/grep.go:90
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The skipDirs map unconditionally skips vendor/ during WalkDir traversal. Go projects using 'go mod vendor' store dependencies in vendor/ and may need to search them. While this matches default behavior of tools like ripgrep, downstream users of this library may need an override mechanism.
Suggested fix: Consider adding a configurable skip list (e.g. via GlobTool/GrepTool struct field) that defaults to the current skipDirs but can be overridden. Alternatively, document the limitation and accept it as a deliberate trade-off matching standard tool behavior.
